title: Circus Entertainment at Southsea Common and King George V Playing Fields, Cosham - 2022
description:
Portsmouth City Council is inviting tenders from suitably qualified suppliers to deliver circuses and associated entertainment at Southsea Common and King George V Playing Fields, Cosham. 
      
      Three events are planned for the 2022 season, two at Southsea Common and one at King George V Playing Fields. Suppliers can bid for one, two of three events. 
      
      Event 1 : Southsea Common - Operating period of up to 2 weeks and 4 days between dates of 15th April  - 2nd May 2022
      Event 2 : Southsea Common - Operating period of up to 2 weeks between dates of 12th September - 26th September 2022
      Event 3 : King George V Playing Fields - Operating period of 1 week between dates of 17th June - 19th August 2022
      
      Historically, the two locations have lent themselves to different types of events.  Southsea Common has typically favoured traditional big top events aimed at a wide audience, with King George V Playing Fields hosting a smaller event aimed at families and younger audiences. With this in mind, the Council is offering each event as a separate Lot.  
      
      Suppliers should clearly indicate which event is the subject of their bid and where necessary, indicate their preference on a ranked basis.
      The Council's preference is to secure a programme that provides a varied offer of events in the City, and as a result of this tender the Council may instruct one, two or three separate suppliers.
      
      The initial Contract period will be for one year with the possibility of extending the term by a further two years to a maximum total term of three years in annual increments to be agreed, subject to the agreement and performance of both parties.
      Events for future years will be determined on an annual basis. The Council cannot provide any commitments or guarantees regarding availability of future events or their dates.
      
      This is a concession opportunity that generates an income to the Council.
      
      Timetable for the procurement is:
      Deadline for clarifications                    Wed 27th October 2021 at 14:00
      Deadline for tender submissions      Wed 10th November 2021 at 14:00
      Award                                                            Mon 22nd November 2021          
      
      Contracts will commence on the 1st January 2022 for the delivery of circuses that year.

title: WDC - Provision of the Development and Production of a Management Plan for Abbey Fields
description:
Warwick District Council wishes to appoint a suitably qualified team to develop and prepare a comprehensive management plan to guide the efficient and effective management of Abbey Fields. 
      
      The need for a management plan is set against a background of significant planned housing growth for Kenilworth identified in the Warwick District Local Plan as adopted in September 2017.  This will see a 20% population increase through nearly 2,000 new homes, as well as the impact of HS2 which passes nearby, and other significant developments such as King's Hill, where approximately 4,000 new homes are planned.  This will bring significant new pressures on existing infrastructure, particularly green space attractions like Abbey Fields.
      
      Despite its popularity and importance, Abbey Fields lacks a clear vision. The park means different things to different people and this can often lead to a disjointed approach to management, maintenance and investment.  Different groups sometimes have contrasting views over what is best for the park. We see this plan as an opportunity to bring together the various stakeholders and establish a clear vision and management structure to take forward Abbey Fields over the next 10 years.
      
      The budget for this project £30,000. There is an additional budget of £15,000 to cover additional survey work.

title: Contract to Procure a Developer for Parcel One at Lion Fields, Kidderminster
description:
Lion Fields is a rare opportunity to deliver the comprehensive redevelopment of the eastern gateway to Kidderminster town centre.

Wyre Forest District Council (WFDC) alongside North Worcestershire Economic Development and Regeneration (NWEDR) are seeking a developer with the skills, experience, financial resources and ambition to deliver a leisure-led mixed use development in Kidderminster. Lion Fields is a site of approximately 6.5 hectares (16 acres) and takes its name from both historic and current references to the locality. It is envisaged that redevelopment will comprise a mix of uses incorporating leisure, retail, residential, creative and community uses.

A Development Framework setting out WFDC's vision for Lion Fields has been adopted in support of redevelopment in this area and a phased approach to delivery is proposed for the entire site.  However, this invitation relates to Parcel One of Lion Fields which extends to approximately 3.10 acres and is offered as an opportunity to bring forward a new mixed use leisure and commercial development to anchor this regeneration area.

The procurement is being carried out via the Restricted route, all documentation relating to the contract can be downloaded via the Due North portal, including the Selection Questionnaire,  http://procontract.due-north.com
Queries relating to the SQ must be sent via the electronic portal Due North and will also be answered via the portal.

The deadline for the submission of the Selection Questionnaire is  2pm 8th August 2017

title: AHS5040(B) Mental Health Accommodation Based Schemes; Cannon Street Road, Ensign Street and Glasshouse Fields
description:
Contract Title:	AHS5040(B) Mental Health Accommodation Based Schemes; Cannon Street Road, Ensign Street and Glasshouse Fields
The London Borough of Tower Hamlets (LBTH) is seeking expressions of interest from interested organisations for the above services and / or goods.

Description:

The borough is seeking to tender the support service at Cannon Street Road, Ensign Street and Glasshouse Fields and is seeking innovative models of service delivery which take into account the specifics of the service being sought and that provides efficient and effective support provision that will enable individuals with severe and enduring mental health needs to develop the life skills required to maintain their independence within the community.    

The key requirements of the specification include:

•	Supporting people towards recovery and independence to enable them to develop the life skills to move on to less supported or step down accommodation.
•	The ability to deliver recovery orientated support approaches.
•	The provision of a support service that is available 24 hours a day 7 days a week
•	Ensuring a minimum of 2 staff are on shift during the core hours of 8am to 11pm.
•	The ability to provide in the region of  7 hours of flexible support to each service user per week  at Cannon Street Road and 5 hours of support to each service user per week at Ensign Street and Glasshouse Fields.. 
•	Effective team leadership and competency in securing clinical confidence in the service.
•	The ability to support the delivery of the Mental Health Strategy 2014-2019 and supporting the achievements and embedding of the Mental Health  Accommodation Strategy 2011 to 2016.
•	Ability to commence the new contract from 1 April 2017

This contract will be based on bidder’s agreement to enter into a Housing Management Agreement with the landlord of Cannon Street Road, Peabody and the Landlord of Glasshouse Fields and Ensign Street, Look Ahead Care, Support and Housing. Failure to agree a Housing Management Agreement will result in the contract not being awarded.

The Council is committed to improving the quality of life for both residents and businesses within Tower Hamlets. In line with The Public Services (Social Value) Act 2012 the authority will consider how this procurement might improve the economic, social and environmental well-being of the relevant area. In order to achieve this, it is our objective to work with existing and future contractors to secure an appropriate level of community benefits through the procurement process. Detailed guidance and minimum levels of the economic and community benefit applicable to this tender will be available within the tender documentation.

The anticipated contract start date is: Saturday, 01 April 2017

The estimated value of the contract is: 	
£350,000 to £371,000 per annum.   An upper threshold of £371,000 has been placed on this tender.	Choose an item.

For a period of: This contract is for a period of 3 years +1 +1 t

title: Assessment of drift exposures to edge of field surface waterbodies
description:
A bespoke drift trial data set to validate the existing work developing the UK drift curve. There is a lack of available field data that assess drift exposures from in field applications. This trial will enable better understanding of the contribution of in-field spray applications to the overall drift exposures in edge of field surface water bodies (and off crop areas).

To support HSE's Aquatic Buffer Zone Review a project was undertaken to develop a UK spray drift exposure curve that was representative of UK practice in arable fields. The outcome of that previous project is a proposed UK drift curve which has been generated based upon available edge of field drift data and modelling output using the Silsoe Spray Drift Model (SiMOD) and this project follows on from that work.
cpvDescription: Research and development services and related consultancy services
cpvDescriptionExpanded: Research and development services and related consultancy services

title: ITT - Variable Field NMR Spectrometer
description:
The National Physical Laboratory [NPL] requirement is for a Variable Field Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) spectrometer for the measurement of T1, T2, T2*, apparent diffusion coefficient, and proton density fat fraction in the liquid state. The purpose is to enable traceable, high-precision measurements of these measurands, to allow for the development of metrological standards for Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI). The ITT is separated into two components; the Instrument, and the Facility. We welcome tenders for either or both. 

The Instrument should comprise an NMR Spectrometer, with all associated componentry (e.g., the magnet, console, probe(s), temperature control system, shims, and workstation). This should be a fully programmable spectrometer, with the capability to measure the NMR spectra of chosen samples using appropriate pulse sequences and export the resulting data to an appropriate data storage or archival facility. The magnetic field should be variable, with the ability to change the field on a monthly basis. The magnetic field should be able to reach clinically relevant MRI fields (e.g., 1.5, 2.9, 3.0, 5.0, 7.0 T), and have the capability for lower field strengths down to 0.1 T or lower. The spectrometer should be compatible with the existing equivalent facility at NIST Boulder.

The Facility should comprise a 'room-within-a-room' NMR Laboratory, including a dedicated power supply. This Facility should be shielded from RF, magnetic, and vibrational interference sufficiently such that a future MRI scanner could be installed adjacent. It should include temperature regulation to ensure a set point of 20C +- 0.5C is always maintainable, with temperature, humidity, and pressure monitoring. The Facility should be access controlled in a manner compatible with the NPL access control system, and include provision for lone working, and appropriate safety features. It must follow all necessary safety regulations, including with regards to signage.

title: Kitchen & Bathroom Works - Exeter City Council
description:
The Council is seeking to appoint a main contractor capable of delivering the entire contract including but not limited to organising works,  managing sub-contractors, site management and carrying out kitchen and bathroom refurbishment works to council housing properties within the Exeter City Council area.

The works are to comprise the rip out and renewal of existing kitchens and bathrooms to occupied council housing properties including but not limited to associated minor alterations to existing layouts, plastering, electrical, heating, plumbing, ventilation, flooring, wall tiling and decorating works.

The contract is proposed to be a JCT Measured Term Contract for a term of 4 years and annually renewed subject to satisfactory performance. The contract value will be in the region of £850,000 in the first year (this shall not be guaranteed) and it is expected that subsequent years if agreed will be of a similar value (again not guaranteed). 

In the first year it is estimated that there will be 160 bathrooms and 60 kitchens and it will be expected that bathrooms will be completed at the rate of 4 bathrooms per week and kitchens completed at the rate of 2 kitchens per week both commencing 1st May 2017. As the volumes cannot be underwritten or guaranteed, bidders should consider how they might develop a flexible operational model.

Those companies that pass the Selection Questionnaire shall be invited to tender.  In addition this initial stage shall also require companies to sign Non-Disclosure Agreements so that TUPE information may be released at the tender stage of this procurement.

title: (NU/1749) - Refurbishment of 11 Shared Kitchens in Bowsden Court
description:
This tender is to establish a Contract for the refurbishment of 11 shared kitchens in Bowsden Court. The University is relying on Contractor's expertise and knowledge of commercial kitchens to identify and recommend requirements, and to provide a full and accurate cost for the Contract.
The scope is:
i. Recommending level of replacement for kitchen doors and carcasses.
ii. Work with Accommodation Services / E&F / the Main Works Contractor to specify final requirements.
iii. Survey the site before fabrication or purchase of any equipment, furniture and fittings to ensure they will fit.
iv. Provide a general arrangement drawing and all necessary information, including services and setting out details, in order for E&F / the Main Contractor to complete his works. E&F / the Main Contractor will terminate all services within 1 metre of the final required connection, using an appropriate outlet, socket or valve.
v. Refurbishment of the kitchens by 31st July 2022 in accordance with the specification in Section 3 of the ITT, with doors and carcasses able to withstand higher levels of moisture and with reduced risk of damage from impacts.
vi. Supply (including unloading from vehicles and moving to area) and installation of any equipment, furniture and fittings. The Contractor will be required to complete all final equipment connections, providing all necessary equipment for the fixing, connecting and full execution of the works as indicated by the drawings and specification as a whole.
vii. Testing and commissioning all equipment.
viii. Remove all waste packaging and re-use/recycle where possible, and if not possible to re-use/recycle dispose of in method that most reduces negative environmental impact.
The refurbishment is part of a larger Works programme by a named Main Works Contractor appointed by the University's Estates and Facilities (E&F)

Before submitting a tender you must attend a site visit. The site visits will take place between the 31st of January 2022 to the 4th of February 2022 between 10am and 4pm. Failure to attend a site visit will result in the tender not being considered further.

title: Kitchen and Bathroom Replacement Programme - Multi Lot - Lot 1
description:
Award Notice. Lot 1

Previous publication: 64db0b16-8ead-4888-9d66-2aa85d19059e

The London Borough of Hillingdon (the Council) invites tenders from suitably qualified and experienced Works Contractors in relation to entering into an agreement to provide Kitchen and Bathroom installation Works for various properties across our housing stock by post code area, for a three (3) year period with the option to extend for a further two (2) years. The Council intends to appoint up to four (4) Works Contractors.

The installation works will be in phased packages, depending on budget approval, and is normally released assuming installations being completed within a 16-week programme.

Contracts will be awarded with regards to the following Lots/Areas within the borough:

Lot 1 - Area North postcodes: UB9, HA6, HA5 (typically 14% of potential volume)
Lot 2 - Area Central postcodes: UB10, HA4, UB8 (typically 30% of potential volume)
Lot 3 - Area South postcodes: UB7, UB4, UB3 (typically 56% of potential volume)
Lot 4 - Back Up Contractor All areas. (Estimate is £0 - £50k per annum. Subject to use.)

The London Borough of Hillingdon does not guarantee any volumes of orders or spend values.

The anticipated total volume of kitchen and bathroom installations is not guaranteed but is estimated to be 40 kitchens & 40 bathrooms per phase.

The London Borough of Hillingdon retains the right to award only one, some, all, or none of the available Lots.

The planned commencement of this contract will be November 2024.

JCT MTC 2016 With amendments.

The Council has concluded a selection process to identify the preferred Kitchen Survey and Kitchen Supplies provider, Howdens Joinery Ltd. Successful Contractors will need to purchase kitchen items from Howdens together with any other materials / items required to complete the installations.

title: Contract 52 - Kitchens & Bathroom Replacement 2021 -2024
description:
The Authority is seeking expressions of interest from suitably qualified and experienced Providers to enter into a framework agreement (Framework) for the delivery of its Kitchen & Bathroom Programmed replacements
Works 2021-2024 (the Works), the provider is to provide the full Scope of Works which is included in the full Tender pack. The Works are to assist with the delivery of the Authority's capital investment plan requiring differing values and complexities as required for the Framework Contract. Successfully awarded Providers will be required to work together with North Tyneside Council (the Authority) to drive improvements and support the aims and ambitions of the Contract.
The Authority will be acting as Principal Contractor and successful Providers will be in capacity of a Sub-Contractor. The Framework arrangement will consist of an initial term of 2.5 years (30 months), plus an option, at the sole discretion of the Authority, of a further 12 months.

It is envisaged that 3 Providers will be appointed to the Framework.

The scope of works includes kitchen replacements, bathroom replacements, internal painting and decorating, removing and installing wall tiling, removal and installing floor coverings, and kitchen electrical upgrades along with all associated works.
Works to be completed in occupied properties.

The Provider is required to undertake the design survey to each property. Detailed Drawings must then be produced for each dwelling using a suitable design software for the kitchen & bathroom. The Provider will then use these to produce a full Bill of Quantities for the scheme. 
All kitchen unit orders must be placed through The Symphony Group PLC unless stated otherwise by The Authority to comply with the Authorities current on going Programme of Works.

Further information can be found once you have registered / expressed an interest via the ProContract (Proactis/NEPO) Portal using the link further below, the tender pack should be available to download immediately after registering an interest, however, you may need to either go back to the home screen or login again and go to 'My Activities' at the top of the Home screen if you are unable to locate them after expressing an interest.

title: Appointment of a Principal Contractor for Construction of a New Sports Pavilion at King George V Playing Fields, Winchester
description:
Winchester City Council (WCC) are seeking a suitably qualified Principal Contractor to construct a new sports pavilion at King George V Playing Fields, Milland Road,
Bar End, Winchester, SO23 0QA.

The greenfield site has been vested in perpetual trust as open land for public 
playing fields. The area north of the access road has been demarcated for a new 
pavilion development. The playing fields are currently served by two sports 
pavilions which do not meet current standards, are not of adequate size and are in a deteriorating state of repair and condition. These existing pavilions are to be 
demolished outside of the main works contract.
 
The project proposes the construction of a modern and compliant two-storey sports
 pavilion which will accommodate: a first-floor clubhouse with a kitchen and 
external open viewing balcony, ground-floor changing rooms with associated 
showers, officials changing, separate toilets, storage, external bike shelter and 
bin store. The pavilion will also have a first-floor balcony viewing area. 
Landscaping includes alterations to the access route, car parking, drainage, paving and installation of EV chargers together with other associated works. 

Stride Treglown Architecture & Design Practice have developed a design which 
is attractive, of high quality, efficient and economical to maintain. It is hoped 
that the new Pavilion will serve the current and future needs of the clubs, players, officials and spectators of all ages, cultures, abilities and gender. 

The works are to be procured and administered under the JCT Design and Build 
Contract 2016. 

Works must be completed on-site by August 2023 in anticipation of the next football season. 

To register for this procurement opportunity and access the documents, please
use the link contained within 'Additional Details' and follow the instructions
1 and 2:
1) Click on "New supplier registration"
2) Complete the Supplier Registration form

If you need any further assistance with registration or have any queries about using the portal, please contact EU Supply as follows:

E-mail: uksupport@eu-supply.com
Telephone: 0800 840 2050 during office hours ONLY (08.00 to 17.00).

title: UK SBS PR19049 3T Horizontal Magnet
description:
**Final date and time for the submission of bids is 16/10/2019 at 11:00 DO NOT apply directly to the buyer. All tender information MUST be submitted through the CCS eSourcing Portal.**

This requirement is for the design, manufacture, delivery and the installation of 3T Horizontal Magnet for the use at UK Research and Innovation(UKRI) - Science & Technology Facilities Council(STFC)  - Rutherford Appleton Laboratory (RAL).

ISIS pulsed neutron and muon source is a world-leading centre for research at STFC - RAL near Oxford, in the UK. Its suite of neutron and muon instruments give unique insights into the properties of materials on the atomic scale. 

Despite the great investment in building beamlines offering polarisation of the neutron beam, ISIS lacks sample environment optimised to exploit the full potential of the techniques. Until now, a set of room temperature electromagnets have been used in experiments requiring polarisation. These set of electromagnets offer a modest maximum field of 1.2 T perpendicular to the beam (even less if a cryostat needs to be added to the experiment) or 0.4 T parallel to the beam, and poor polarisation transport at low fields. This is a region of the utmost importance since most of the applications will work in absence or very small fields. Therefore for fully exploiting this appealing property of the neutron beams, ISIS needs to make an effort in expanding the available sample environment in order to provide larger fields and better polarisation transport at low fields. 

Therefore a 3T horizontal magnet which transports the polarisation is required which will tackle both issues at once: it will multiply the maximum field by a factor or 2.5 (a factor 7.5 parallel to the beam), meaning that less experiments would have to be redesigned to accommodate the existing sample environment instead of the optimum scientific case; and the increase of the polarisation transport at low fields will allow the users to explore the regions closer to the working conditions for many prototype devices.

Detailed 3T Horizontal Magnet requirements are defined in the attached document,UK SBS PR19049 Specification.

title: 2879/510/JS/COM/20 - High-speed temperature field measurement camera/sensor
description:
2879/510/JS/COM/20 - High-speed temperature field measurement camera/sensor

The University of Sheffield wishes to invite tenders for a 'High-speed temperature field measurement camera/sensor' on behalf of the Advanced Manufacturing Research Centre, Castings Group at Catcliffe, South Yorkshire.

Scope of Requirement


The ITT can be downloaded by registering and expressing your interest on the University`s e-tendering system https://in-tendhost.co.uk/Sheffield

If you have any questions or comments in rA high-speed temperature field measurement device is required to understand the thermal and thermo-mechanical characteristics of high-speed impact, machining, laser ablation and other highly transient thermal events that occur during the manufacture and testing of next-generation advanced engineering materials including composites and hybrid materials. To characterise the role of thermal energy during such events, a sensor, in the form of an Infra Red (IR) detector is required. This needs to be capable of high data-capture rates (at least 10kHz under windowed resolution) of temperature field data in the  IR spectrum. The sensor should provide high-resolution, high-fidelity temperature data within the field of view that allows the operator to measure large thermal gradients in a single temperature field and temperature changes over small periods of time - clearly and accurately.elation to this tender they must be submitted via the In-tend System, this can be accessed at https://in-tendhost.co.uk/Sheffield

title: Field Staff Support Service Solution Phase 1
description:
The Office for National Statistics (the Contracting Authority) has chosen to adopt a 'Hybrid' model for the Field Staff Support Service, combining 'In-House' and supplier support for query routing:
•	Tier 0 - ONS to develop and maintain a Self Help facility with a searchable set of Frequently Answered Questions (FAQs. The FAQs will need to be maintained and updated over time based on real queries received by the support team, and backed up by 'how to guides' and the material used in training). 
•	Tier 1 - ONS establish a Field Force Support Services (FFSS) team with resources dedicated to answering simple queries and routing more complex queries. 
•	Tier 2 - Escalation of complex queries to Tier 2 will be managed by the Tier 1 team. The supplier may refer 'policy' level issues back to the Contracting Authority for resolution.

This requirement is to provide consultancy support to develop the design, implementation plan, resource plan and costing for the Tier 1 Field Force Contact Centre and will be provided over two phases of work.  This contract is for Phase 1 - Gap Analysis and Requirements.

Phase 1 - Gap Analysis and Requirements

Gap Analysis
•	Conduct an assessment of the work conducted so far by the Contracting Authority's resources with a particular focus on strategy, process, technology, organisational design and people capability. This will include a desktop review of available documents, interviews and working sessions with the Contracting Authority's resources. 
•	Analyse the requirements/services to be provided by the third parties delivering the Census (from the Invitation to Tender documentation)
•	Define the high level requirements for the Tier 1 ONS Field Staff Support Service
•	Based on available data and assumptions developed with ONS regarding call handling time, call volumes by type and timings develop high level options for the call centre operations.
•	Conduct a working session to discuss and agree the options to be taken into design phase.

Requirements
•	Review and provide an independent assessment of the current requirements that have been developed by the ONS for the Tier 1 ONS Field Staff Support Service
•	Where needed, further develop the high level requirements for the Tier 1 ONS Field Staff Support Service
•	Based on available data and assumptions developed with ONS regarding call handling time, call volumes by type and timings develop high level options for the call centre operations.
•	Conduct working session to discuss and agree the options to be taken into design phase.
•	Draft a report that outlines the approach to be taken into detailed design
•	Develop a detailed project plan and resource profile for Phase 2 (if this is to be pursued).

description:

The proposal is to refurbish the Market Hall kitchen with the intention to retain certain elements.
The wall cupboards and the cooker are to be retained (except window wall) and incorporated into the new kitchen. We are inviting quotations for the following
• Remove all kitchen base units and the wall cupboard adjacent the window only and dispose

paper towel dispenser, fire blanket, first aid kit, clock

title: The Supply, Installation, Maintenance and The Right to Display Advertising on Bus Shelters in Swindon Borough
description:
The Supply, Installation, Maintenance and The Right to Display Advertising on Bus Shelters in Swindon Borough  

Swindon Borough Council invites expressions of interest from suitably qualified and experienced service providers for the provision of the Supply, Installation, Maintenance and The Right to Display Advertising on Bus Shelters in Swindon Borough. 

 

Project Description 

This will be a concession contract under which the successful supplier will generate income to fund these service requirements from selling advertising space on the bus shelters. The Council will require a share of advertising income from the supplier for this contract. The supplier will be expected to provide new bus shelters at new locations and replace bus shelters on a regular renewal programme. There are currently 368 bus shelters altogether, of which the Council own 35. The remaining 333 bus shelters are owned by an existing supplier, who are anticipated to remove their bus shelters over a period of 6 months following the end of their contract.  

 

There will be an additional 23 bus shelters situated in Fleming Way, installed by a different supplier, and owned by the Council. Once installed, the Fleming Way bus shelters will form part of the contract agreement for cleaning and advertising. 

 

 

The successful supplier will have to replace these bus shelters if they are removed by the existing supplier at the same time of their removal. Swindon Borough Council own a number of bus shelters which it will continue to own and these will be part of the contract. In addition, the Council will be purchasing new bus shelters for the new Fleming Way interchange and these shelters will become part of this contract once installed, although they will remain owned by the Council. Bus shelters owned by parish councils or developers or other parties are not part of this contract. 

 

The supplier will clean and maintain the bus shelters as part of a regular maintenance programme.   

 

The initial contract term will be for 180 months with an option to extend for a further 60 months for a potential total contract term of 240 months as stated in the Invitation To Tender (ITT) documents. The fifteen-year estimated potential advertising income value is between £0m to £75m to the supplier. The extension period would be 5 years with an estimate potential advertising income between £0m to £25m. 

 

Full details relating to the Services are provided in the tender documents. 

 

To participate, Suppliers will need to Register as a Supplier with ProContract, www.supplyingthesouthwest.org.uk, then Register an Interest before obtaining access to the tender documents. In the event of difficulties registering as a supplier, please refer to the System Administrator Proactis support team ProContractSuppliers@proactis.com 

 

Tender clarifications to be submitted in writing via Messaging in ProContract tender portal by 16th October 2024, 1400 hrs UK Local time. The Council's responses

title: Invitation to Tender for Bus Shelter Advertising Supply/Erection and Maintenance (Option A) and Advertising Only (Option B).
description: Ipswich is the county town of Suffolk with a population of around 137,500 within the Borough, expanding to around 350,000 within a 15 mile radius. A thriving urban area, Ipswich is well provisioned with shops, parks, leisure and cultural facilities that help to make it a regional centre within East Anglia. Sitting at the point where the A14 and A12 meet and the Great Eastern Mainline connects with other regional railway lines, Ipswich is well connected to the wider world and is also a leading area of public and private employment with particular emphasis on the insurance/finance, IT/creative and logistics sectors. The primary purpose of this tender is for the provision of a number of high quality bus shelters for the bus passengers of Ipswich, financed through the exclusive provision of advertising upon the said shelters. Two options are proposed as the methods of delivery and bidders are required to submit bids for both. Option A sees the bidder supply, install and maintain the advertising style bus shelters. Bidders are required to state the number of such shelters that they would supply and the amount they would pay to the Council per such shelter per year. This would be increased throughout the agreement in line with the Retail Prices Index. Under this option, the Council has a sub-option during the agreement for the successful bidder to clean and/or maintain Council owned bus shelters as well (which do not carry advertising) for which bidders must indicate a cost to the Council per shelter per year. This would also be increased throughout the agreement in line with the Retail Prices Index. In both cases, financial penalties would apply for poor performance. Under this option, bidders are required to submit as many designs (including detailed technical drawings) for advertising style bus shelters as they wish, provided that at least one design is submitted for shelters in conservation areas and outside listed buildings (a ‘special’ design) and one for other locations (a ‘standard’ design). A single design can be submitted for both categories if desired. All designs must meet the Council’s Specification. Option B sees the Council supply, install and maintain the advertising style bus shelters (with financial penalties for poor performance). The bidder is required to sell the advertising spaces and arrange for the advertising to be provided and changed as required. Bidders would pay a fixed amount to the Council per shelter per year. This would be increased throughout the agreement in line with the Retail Prices Index. Bidders are required to state the amount that they are prepared to pay to the Council per shelter per year and the number of advertising style bus shelters that they are prepared to pay this amount for. For both Option A and Option B, bidders are required to submit bids for a 10 year and a 15 year agreement, during which time the bidder would have exclusive advertising rights on all the bus shelters provided under the agreement,

The new Contract will consist of upgrading, and maintaining the city's public transport infrastructure and associated advertising within bus shelters and on Information Panels that will deliver income generation and improve public transport facilities in line with The Council's Corporate Plan - https://www.plymouth.gov.uk/aboutcouncil/corporateplan, Plymouth Plan - https://www.plymouth.gov.uk/planningandbuildingcontrol/plymouthplan and the wider Joint Local Plan - https://www.plymouth.gov.uk/sites/default/files/JLPAdoptedVersion.pdf.

In return for the exclusive right to erect bus shelters and information panels on the highway, and the exclusive right to sell and install advertising on such bus shelters and information panels for the term of the Contract, the Council expect that this Contract will generate a Fixed Fee and Revenue Share payment per advertising bus shelter and information panel advertising face on which advertising consent is granted.

title: Winter Shelter
description:
We are looking to be able to use a building for a night shelter for up to 30 residents - to be available as soon as is practicable.

Rough sleeping has risen dramatically in the city since 2013, Last year the Rough Sleeper service had contact with 951 people who were street homeless. One of the most crucial steps in helping someone to find a route off the streets is to help them access a shelter.  There are 80 shelter beds in the city and an additional 42 beds open during the winter but in the last snapshot count in July, there were still another 130 people sleeping on the streets. There is a clear need for more shelter beds in the city.  

The shelter provision would need to be able to provide accommodation space that could be only available as a night shelter (10pm - 8am) or  could provide 24 hour staffing provision.

Likely to be dorm style - but depends on building available.  As a minimum it will need to provide:

•	access to toilets and basic shower facilities 
•	simple kitchenette area for making drinks
•	some communal & basic storage space (eg to locate lockers )

The building needs to meet building regulations for residential use.  The cost of any required building work needs to be clarified in proposal and indication of whether this will be an additional pressure on funding requirement.

Dependant on existing planning consent on the building use, new planning approval to run as a shelter may be required.

Proposed staffing structure would need to be clarified in your submission - to include proposals on how volunteers could be utilised.  

The needs of people using the shelter will be assessed to minimise community disruption before referral to the shelter.

Housing benefit may be applicable in certain circumstances.
We are looking to get a shelter up and running as soon as possible - opening this winter and funded until at least March 31st 2021
n.b.  If this generates sufficient interest from the market we will need to consider going out for a formal process to purchase this service.

title: Expressions of Interest for Bus Shelter Operating Contract - Swale Borough Council Area
description:
Expressions of Interest for Bus Shelter Operating Contract - Swale Borough Council Area

This opportunity closes at 12:00 noon on Tuesday 1st July.

This Notice provides information of a future opportunity to provide a bus shelter supply, operation and maintenance service for bus shelter locations and infrastructure in the Swale Borough area in Kent.

Swale Borough Council (SBC) would like to engage with bus shelter providers in regard to supply, installation and maintenance of bus shelters, including some possible living roof shelters, for bus shelter infrastructure managed by SBC in the Swale area and to agree a preferred supplier and product as per SBC's specification.

SBC requests that interested suppliers express an interest in this opportunity and share their thoughts and expertise to assist SBC to determine and shape the specification. The solution can be a new design or based on an existing design used elsewhere. Integrated advertising within the shelters can be traditional posters or, subject to location agreement, digital media screens.

Providers that are interested but are not registered on the ProContract Portal should register at the following address in the first instance: https://procontract.due-north.com

The Council intends to hold a competitive tendering process during 2025/26, to secure these new bus shelters. It is the Council's aim to make the procurement process as easy as possible for bidders to complete. It is anticipated that the initial contract of supply and maintenance will run for approximately 5 years with the provision for an extension of up to 5 years, and a commencement date of April 2026.

Your expression of interest must be made via the ProContract Portal, by 12:00 noon on Tuesday 1st July.

We would be particularly interested to hear whether you think there is any potential for income generation for the Council through advertising, or whether there is scope to provide living roof shelters at particular locations. 

Please submit comments via the ProContract messaging function.

title: Contract for the Supply, Installation and Maintenance of Bus Shelters and Information Panels, including right to Display Advertising on Bus Shelters  - AWARD
description:
Hampshire County Council invites applications from suitably experienced organisations who wish to be considered for selection to tender for the Supply, Installation and Maintenance of Bus Shelters and Information Panels, including right to Display Advertising on Bus Shelters.
      
      The Authority is seeking a concessionaire to provide repair, maintenance and installation of climate friendly bus shelters and to display advertising on the paper and digital shelter panels for a return of profit share.
      
      This will initially be in the Boroughs of Gosport , Hart and Rushmoor with scope for further areas to be encompassed into this contract.
      The remaining 8 Borough Councils in the Hampshire County council area can fall in scope for this contract , however there is no guarantee that they will do so . The numbers and locations of the shelters can change throughout the term of the contract and there is no guarantee of additional shelters being included in this tender. The estimation of value for the three localities is £35,334,000.00 over the 15 year term based on Authority spend on advertising. Further details can be found in the procurement documents.
      
      The shelters will be owned and maintained under the responsibility of the successful concessionaire. 
      
      The Contract will commence on 18th July 2024 and will be for a period of ten years with the option to extend for a further five years.
      
      Interested parties should register their interest in the project via the In-Tend website https://in-tendhost.co.uk/hampshire, and complete and submit the tender return document together with any supporting information requested by 14.00 hrs on Tuesday the 28th May 2024.  
      
      How to obtain the Tender Documents
      The Tender documents can be accessed when logged into In-Tend by selecting 'Tenders' then 'Current Tenders' and 'View Details' on the relevant tender advert and clicking the 'Express Interest' tab. Once in the tender there are five tabs - 'Tender', 'ITT - documents', 'Correspondence', 'Clarifications' and 'History'. Select the 2nd tab (ITT - documents) where you will find useful information under 'Tender Details' continue to scroll down to the heading 'Tender Documents Received' where you will be able to view / download the documents.
      
      Please download all the documents and read very carefully.
      
      Opting In and Opting Out
      Please note you are required to 'Opt In' before you can access the 'My tender Return' to start populating your response. 
      
      PLEASE NOTE you are required to 'Opt In' to view & complete the two Questionnaires for this project. 
      
      The 'Opt Out' functionality will also be available throughout the duration of the tender process. Opting out will give you the option of declaring you no longer want to receive any further communication in relation to this tender along with the opportunity of providing comments and feedback for this decision.
      
      You can choose to 'Opt In' at any time during the ten...

title: Bus Shelter Advertising Extension
description:
Nottinghamshire County Council has extended its existing concession agreement for bus shelter advertising by three years to support the transition of transport functions to EMCCA is completed. 

Nottinghamshire County Council (the Council) maintains in excess of 4,500 bus stops with 1,483 bus shelters and almost 1,200 real time displays. Since 2000, the Incumbent Provider has supplied and maintained 470 of the 1,483 bus shelters in Nottinghamshire at no cost to the Council in exchange for selling advertising space on approximately 45% of their shelters and retaining ownership of each bus shelter it supplies under the contract. This represents excellent value to the Council as it would cost over £6.5 million to install like for like shelters and £50k per annum to clean and maintain them.

Since 2019, the Incumbent Provider started replacing its paper advertising with digital advertising and the Council negotiated a contract variation to receive an income for every digital panel added to the Incumbent Provider's estate. The Incumbent Provider currently have 30 such panels, with the income used to offset the Council's bus stop infrastructure maintenance costs. The Incumbent Provider supplies and has maintained its shelter estate to a high standard, and it remains in good condition.

The current contract for delivery of the bus shelter advertising will expire on 1 May 2025, and it has been in place for 25 years.

The Council's Local Transport Authority functions will transfer to the East Midlands Combined County Authority (EMCCA) on or before 31st March 2026. At present it is unclear whether bus shelters owned by the Council will transfer or contracts such as that held between the Council and the Incumbent Provider will be novated by this date or at all. There is also expected to be a considerable period of adjustment within EMCCA as the Transport team beds in and the functions to support delivery mature.

noticeIdentifier: Bus Shelter Advertising and Maintenance Concession
title: Soft Market Engagement - Bus Shelter Advertising and Maintenance Concession Contract
description:
West Northamptonshire Council will shortly be seeking tenders for a concession contract for the provision and maintenance of bus shelters throughout West Northamptonshire, including Northampton and Daventry, funded from advertising on shelters.

The Council wants to ensure its proposals are designed to enable prospective operators to maximise the value they can achieve, and thus offer to the Council. Its current thinking is:

•	The Operator will use its expertise to provide and maintain shelters.
•	The Council will meet the cost of providing new shelters.
•	The Operator will advertise on shelters in order to generate income, including to maintain shelters. 
•	If the Council requires new shelters or changes to shelters it would carry the financial consequences of this.
•	The Council would share benefit from the net income.

It is unavoidable that there will need to be some control over the location and nature of advertising, but the Council intends to keep this as simple and clear as possible, and avoid the Council becoming involved in decisions about the merits of different advertisements.

The Council will be presenting its proposals via an online meeting on Friday 1st December at 10.30am - 12noon via Teams

To confirm your attendance at this meeting, please email procurement@westnorthants.gov.uk 

If you are unable to attend the meeting, please use the same email address to request a recording. 

Comments on the proposals are sought, and should be sent to the same email address.

This soft market testing exercise is not an invitation to tender or request for formal expressions of interest.  The Council is issuing this request for information and market research only.

title: HCC 03/16 - Provision of Bus Shelters and Bus Stop Cleaning and Maintenance in Hertfordshire
description:
Hertfordshire County Council Highways Department are currently out to tender for the provision of Bus Shelters and Bus Stop Cleaning and Maintenance in Hertfordshire.  Therefore the Council is seeking tenders from suitably qualified and experienced organisations for the supply, installation and maintenance of bus shelters (i.e. advertising and non-advertising) and the generation of revenue sharing from the placing of advertising on these shelters.  

This procurement has been divided into 3 Lots:-

Lot 1 - The Provision, Maintenance and Cleaning of Advertising and Non-Advertising Shelters at specified sites across the County of Hertfordshire (public concession contract and shall be at nil cost to the Council).
Lot 2 - The Provision of Regular Bus Stop Maintenance and Cleaning Services at both Non-Advertising Shelter and Non-Shelter sites across the County of Hertfordshire (public contract).
Lot 3 - The Provision, Maintenance and Cleaning of Advertising, Non-Advertising Shelters and Non-Shelter sites across the County of Hertfordshire (a combination of Lots 1 and 2 above).

Please note that the Council can award any Lot but there is no intention to award all 3 Lots.  For the avoidance of doubt, Tenderers can tender for any of the Lots, i.e. on the basis of the following:-
- Lot 1 only
- Lot 2 only
- Lots 1 and 2 (both individually)
- Lot 3 (a combination of Lot 1 and 2 combining the commercial benefits)

Submitting a tender for one Lot does not exclude the same Tenderer from submitting a tender for the other Lots.  

In view of the Lots and subject to the successful completion of the procurement process, the Council intends to award a public concession contract and a public contract. This procurement is a one stage procurement process.  Suppliers wishing to take part in this project are invited to “express interest” which will give access to the full procurement  documents in the e-tendering system. 

To be considered as a Tenderer you must complete and submit a Bid by the deadline of 12.00 Noon on 16th May 2016.

Any questions relating to this procurement must be made via correspondence on the website, in accordance with the procurement documents and can be addressed to the main contact as shown in the details above. Please allow sufficient time to make your return as late returns will not be permitted by the system. 

Tenderers should be aware that due to the nature of the Services provided, any Contract formed as a result of this procurement process shall be executed as a deed and attested under the common seal of the Council. Tenderers should seek independent legal advice on the implications of this prior to submitting their Tender, where appropriate.

Please also note that the TUPE Staffing Information for this procurement (i.e. Lot 2 and the same requirements for Lot 3 respectively) have been provided along with other documentation for this procurement. TUPE related i...

title: Bus Shelter Installation
description:
To provide the following for the installation of a bus shelter which will be provided pre-built from supplier and delivered to site, and installed on a grassed verge area adjacent to the footway.

•	Provide RAM's and insurance details for the proposed works.
•	Erect site fencing using barriers and signage to ensure the area is always safe while our works are undertaken.
•	CAT scan the proposed area for any live services that maybe located beneath the surface. If found report findings to the client for further investigation.
•	Remove existing bench and remove offsite. Remove existing bus stop sign post and relocate to new position once shelter has been installed. Location to be confirmed.
•	Mark and Set out proposed location of bus shelter. 
•	Excavate and reduce proposed bus shelter base including excavate for new post foundations. All spoil excavated to be removed off site.
•	Supply and fit new concrete footings for new posts. 
•	Supply labour to assist of delivery of bus shelter and lower into place. 
•	Install concrete around each post to position.
•	Supply and fit new edgings around the shelter for new slab surface.
•	Install new subbase and compact.
•	Install new concrete base to bus shelter area.
•	Backfill and around the area with spoil onsite.
•	Tidy all areas and remove any fencing ready for use.

 Quotations including company details to be emailed to alison.blanchard@yateley-tc.gov.uk

title: Bus Shelter Advertising Contract
description: North East Lincolnshire Council is procuring a replacement contract to provide and maintain, which includes cleaning, 84 commercial bus shelter sites in North East Lincolnshire. The successful provider must be able to provide all 84 bus shelter sites no later than six months after the contract commencement date, which is the 30th April 2016. At a minimum, the bus shelter types must be provided on a like-for-like basis, and the provider must cover all costs associated with the provision of the bus shelters. 58 of these currently have capacity for advertising. There are two options for bidders to choose to bid for. Option 1 which is the councils preferred option asks the provider to offer a share of the bus shelters with advertising capacity, which the council can use to place or sell advertisements. Option two has this requirement removed. Option 2 submissions will only be evaluated by the council if there are no compliant submissions for option 1. If there are compliant submissions for option 1, the bids for option 2 will be rejected. The council is also asking bidders to consider if they can deliver a desirable requirement which is to clean the council owned bus shelters on a quarterly basis. Submissions that indicate they only want to deliver the desirable requirement, and do not wish to deliver the essential requirements will be deemed non-complaint and will be rejected. The council requires a percentage of the bus shelters with advertising capacity to place or sell advertisements. The duration of the agreement will be 300 months, with the option to extend for a further 2 x 12 month periods. The contract will not be open to other purchasers. This is a concessions contract. Lots were considered but due to the value, size and location, Lots were not deemed appropriate.

title: Contract for the Supply, Installation, Maintenance and Repair of Bus Shelters
description:
The Council has approximately 730 bus shelters that come under its direct ownership and responsibility. Bus shelters are spread over both urban and rural settings covering all 12 districts of Lancashire. There is programme of works to maintain repair and replace bus shelters. The bus shelters are required to meet set design criteria stipulated by the Public and Integrated Transport service. The number of replacements will likely increase over the contract term as a schedule of replacements is further developed and refined with the successful Tenderer. The new bus shelters will be of varying size but will be required to meet set design criteria and standards as further detailed in the Scope. 

As well as installation of new bus shelters, the Contractor will be required to provide maintenance of bus shelters on a reactive basis as and when the Council requires.   Reactive maintenance includes call-outs to replace side or roof panels, make safe broken glass or structural damage, fix lighting, and undertake cleaning as required.

title: LPS CORP - Contract for the Supply, Installation, Maintenance and Repair of Bus Shelters - RFQ 15244497
description:
Lancashire County Council has approximately 795 bus shelters that come under its direct ownership and responsibility. Bus shelters are spread over both urban and rural settings covering all 12 districts of Lancashire. The Council is procuring a contractor for the supply, installation, maintenance and repair of bus shelters across the Lancashire region. The contract will be for an initial term of 2 years, with the option to extend for up to a further 2 years. 

There is an initial requirement to install approximately 32 new replacement bus shelters per annum. The number of replacements will likely increase over the contract term as a schedule of replacements is further developed and refined with the successful contractor. The new bus shelters will be of varying size but will be required to meet set design criteria and standards which will be further detailed in the Scope. 

As well as installation of new bus shelters, the contractor will be required to provide maintenance of bus shelters on a reactive basis as and when the Council requires. Reactive maintenance includes call-outs to replace side or roof panels, make safe broken glass or structural damage, fix lighting, and undertake cleaning as required. 

The procurement will be undertaken using the Open Procedure.

title: Phase 10 Sheltered Housing Electrical Fire Safety Upgrade
description:
The London Borough of Hillingdon intends to upgrade Fire Detection to LD1 and replace existing electrical consumer units within Sheltered Housing Dwellings.

1.	1-26 Childs Court, This is a sheltered housing Scheme consisting of 26 Dwellings (26No. Internal One bed dwellings)

2.	1-37 Darrell Charles Court, This is a sheltered housing scheme consisting of 37 Dwellings (37No.Internal One bed dwellings)

3.	1-25 Drayton Court, This is a sheltered housing scheme consisting of 23 Dwellings (23No.Internal One bed dwellings)

4.	1-25 Yiewsley Court, This is a sheltered Housing scheme consisting of 25 Dwellings (25No. Internal One bed dwellings)

5.	1-76 Kelf Grove, This is a sheltered Housing scheme consisting of 50 Dwellings (50No.External One bed dwellings)

6.	2-79 Langworth Drive, This is a sheltered Housing scheme consisting of 79 Dwellings (79No.One bed external dwellings)

7.	1-147 Cobden Close, This is a sheltered Housing scheme consisting of 66 Dwellings (66No.One bed external dwellings)

noticeIdentifier: Invitation to Tender for Cabman's Shelter Painting
title: Invitation to Tender for Cabman's Shelter Paintwork
description:
1.	Background
The Cabmen's Shelter currently rests in the East Corner of the Market Place Ripon. It is owned by Ripon City Council following donation from the Civic Society. 

The Listed Building number is 1393142. It is a Grade 2 Listed Building. The reasons it is listed, are as follows:- It has a highly decorative Edwardian design dating from 1911. It is a nationally rare and well-preserved example of a cabmen's shelter, an important reminder of the importance of horse-drawn transport in the early C20, supplied by the well-known firm of Boulton and Paul of Norwich. 

The structure of the Shelter remains in good order after its full restoration in 2019, however general wear & tear and its exposure to the elements are evident on some of the external paintwork.  This is most noticeable on the Southern Elevation where the entrance is located and more generally around the lower boards of the Shelter.

2.	Our requirements
Restoration of exterior paintwork specification:  

External paintwork to be rubbed down, remove all loose and flaking paint. 

Repair work to minor chips or gouges on the external structure. (see example in 'West Elevation Left Hand Side Damage' image in the Appendix 1)

If required, reseal the horizontal joint between the vertical timber frame and the sill with an oil-based mastic to prevent water penetration. 

Prime all bare areas, apply one undercoat and coat gloss oil paint to match the existing colours. 

The work is to be completed with Cabman Shelter in-situ.  

The work is to be completed during summer/Autumn 2025 season. 

We expect the tender to include:
 
•	Provision of all materials for exterior painting, using recognised and certified parts only

•	Method Statement for completion of works including process to be used for painting the Cabman Shelter in-situ. 

We are looking for a reputable and qualified company who can provide us with value for  money.

title: Market Testing - Advertising bus shelters - Basingstoke and Deane
description:
Short description. 

Basingstoke and Deane Borough Council is currently under contract with Clear Channel UK (CCUK) for the operation, repair, and maintenance of a mix of advertising and non-advertising bus shelters at specific locations in the borough. All shelters covered by this specific agreement are owned by CCUK. 

The current agreement concludes in July 2022 and the council is seeking to determine if there is sufficient market interest and capacity for the operation of a similar agreement; and if so the markets views in the potential make up and financial models of such an arrangement. 

Description of the procurement. 

The council is looking to the market to identify the level of interest of providers to deliver a service which will provide an operation, repair, and maintenance programme for approximately 100 bus shelters at current specified locations; 56 of which are currently free of advertising. 

As all shelters are owned by CCUK any successful provider will be expected to negotiate transfer of title as appropriate and be responsible for all associated costs including the maintenance of necessary advertising consents and planning permissions as required by law, as well as insurance and business rates liability relating to each bus shelter.  

Interested providers would be given an exclusive right over bus shelter advertising (other than that associated with local bus service operations) within the borough and as such would be expected to operate the contract on a concessionary basis.  

It is the expectation that any interested provider would operate the contract on an income sharing arrangement. With the provider expected to pay the Council a fee, in return for the exclusive advertising rights. The Council is therefore seeking the markets views on the feasibility, practicality, and potential interest in this contract, particularly ideas and feedback on potential scope of the profit share arrangement.  

 Any company with serious interest in this opportunity should contact the Council via email on procurement@basingstoke.gov.uk with a brief explanation as to their company's interest and any relevant feedback on the potential contractual arrangements outlined above. Responses must be received by 5pm Monday 28th February 2022.  

Please note the Council is merely taking this opportunity to test the market and seek out the market's views as to the viability of providing these concessionary services, and possible options for doing so from July 2022. This is not a procurement exercise and therefore the Council is under no obligation to commit to the appointment of any interested provider.

title: Rebranding of all bus stops and bus shelters in Tranche 2 area in Bee Network branding
description:
TfGM is looking to appoint a contractor to produce and install Bee Network rebranding material at bus shelters and bus stops across Bury, Rochdale, Oldham and parts of North Manchester. This is Tranche 2 of the bus franchising project.

The requirement is for the supplier to print the vinyls for both the shelters and the stops and then to install these new vinyls on the required locations after first removing the old vinyls that are currently there.

The chosen Supplier must be able to start these works as soon as possible. The installation side of the works can take place after the March deadline but must be completed by end of January 24 at the latest.

Design print of new vinyls for 3865 bus stop plates and 1390 shelter cases. Works will involve installation of 2193 plate vinyls only.
The works will include a number of different bus shelter casing types that will need changing - the supplier must print the vinyl to the following size / design and then trim down the vinyl before installation, depending on the type of case that they are working on. The vinyl print dimensions for the bus shelter casings are 1300m x 870mm with a logo size of 423mm x 470mm.
Shelter cases to be removed vinyled and reinstalled. Security torque pin equipment will be required to do this but that will be provided by TfGM to the winning Supplier.
Bus stop poles vary between 3m and 4m in height and the bus stop plate that will need vinyling sits at the top of this.
PDF proofs are to be made available to TfGM for review and approval prior to installation along with an in-person presentation of the standard of work in advance of contract award.

title: Fleming Way Improvements Bus Shelters
description:
Fleming Way Improvements Bus Shelters\r
Swindon Borough Council invites tenders from suitably qualified and experienced contractors for the provision of 24 high quality bus shelters to service 27 bus stops along Fleming Way with the aim of significantly improving the experience of bus users in the Fleming Way area\r
The aspiration is to provide low carbon bus shelters in terms of manufacturing processes materials lifecycle of the product and eventual recycling of materials\r
The bus shelters must be of high quality contemporary but simple design and something for Swindon to be proud of\r
The bus shelters should be as environmentally friendly as possible in their manufacture materials and whole life costs  \r
It is essential to provide units which are low carbon with sustainable methods of construction and established green company credentials in line with the ethos of this project\r
Full details relating to the provision of the bus shelters are supplied in the tender documents\r
To participate Suppliers will need to Register as a Supplier with ProContract then Register an Interest before obtaining access to the tender documents\r
The contract will be initially for a period of 24 months commencing 19th October 2023 as per the contract terms and conditions\r
It is envisaged that the opportunity will range from 900000 to 1500000\r
Tender Responses are required to be submitted no later than Monday 7th August 2023 1200 hrs UK Local time via ProContract tender portal\r
If you require any clarifications on the tender documents please submit these in writing via Messaging in ProContract tender portal by Monday 31st July 2023 1200 hrs UK Local time \r
The Councils responses to these clarification questions will be uploaded periodically on the ProContract tender portal\r
No Tender submissions will be considered without completing the tender documents in full including attachments and returning them in alignment with the instructions within the tender documents

description:

Systems (RTPI) and Associated Services. This is a national Framework Agreement and will provide coverage to local and wider areas of the UK via access to a range of various competent Suppliers.  

 

Depending upon the number and nature of offers received for the Framework Agreement, it is envisaged that a number of Suppliers will be included in the Framework Agreement to provide national geographical coverage and Customer choice. 

 

The Framework Agreement will be split into two (2) LOTs;

This LOT is for the supply of bus shelters, canopies and walkways 

 

Bus Shelters  

Bus stations  

Canopies and walkways  

Trolley and cycle shelters   

 

Made from but not limited to the following materials: 

Aluminum 

Bamboo   

Eco-friendly 

Fabric 

Glazed

Polycarbonate  

Stainless steel

Electricity connections and wi-fi    

Managed service solution

Maintenance, cleaning and upkeep  

Project management   

Refurbishment of existing infrastructure  

 

 

 

LOT 2 - Real Time Passenger Information Systems (RTPI) and Associated Services 

 

Real Time Passenger Information (RTPI) is a means of providing electronic travel information to users of public transport in "real time" via a range of sources. Enabling better journey planning, minimising delay and improving communities access to information on public transport.  

   

Via this Framework Agreement Suppliers are to be able to offer a wide range of different displays suitable for  

  

Bus stops   

Bays  

Terminals  

Onboard vehicles  

Stations   

Vehicles  

Control rooms  

Smartphone apps  

Web applications   

  

Technologies to be available to include but not limited to RGB/LED and LCD.  

  

Each solution will be bespoke to the Customers needs, features to include voice announcement multi-languages, braille and text descriptions, boarding and exiting signals, maps, destination information, content information management.

Branding   

Bus stop infrastructure    

Design and planning   

Electricity connections and wi-fi    

End user sup

description:

      Bus shelters must be appropriate for the local environment into which they are to be installed.  They must be strong, robust and durable and capable of withstanding typical weather conditions with a high resistance to vandalism
      Shelters should also be capable of accommodating Real Time Passenger Information (RTPI) displays which will provide live information on bus arrival times.  All shelters should meet DDA specification (Equalities Act 2010) and be safe, secure and fit for use on the highway.
      Delivery and installation of bus shelters, including any associated groundworks is also required. 
      We identify up to 5 existing bus shelters which will need to be removed and replaced.

title: GB-Bristol: Timber framed shelter - Westonbirt Arboretum Coppice
description:
We will be awarding a contract for the design and build of a new timber framed coppice shelter located in Silk Wood at Westonbirt Arboretum.
      The new shelter will help us continue the critical programme of woodland management and secure the future of coppicing at Westonbirt. The new shelter will provide cover for our coppicers which will enable them to undertake tasks in all weather and allow them to store materials and products associated with the coppice. 
      The Contractor is required to design and build a new shelter which will be made from timber grown and milled at Westonbirt. The shelter will be similar in style to other buildings at the arboretum such as the Silkwood Barn and the Westonbirt Woodworks. The building will be of oak frame construction, traditionally built with a boarded timber roof.

The focus of this contract is for Fastrack RTI display requirements however this may also be used for other bus RTI displays across Kent and Medway to be purchased directly by KCC as and when the need arises.\r
\r
It is proposed that the contract(s) will automatically be extended by 12 months on an annual rolling basis unless otherwise terminated under the provisions of the contract.

title: C21279 - Industrial Cleaning Services of Detection Technology Shelters in Northern France
description:
The Buyer is the Home Office acting through Border Force.

It is a Health and Safety requirement that operatives work in a clean and safe working environment.  To maintain this environment the search shelters need to be cleaned at all ports in Northern France annually.  This will satisfy the French and UK Union Regulation, providing assurance that shelters are fit for purpose through a thorough industrial clean.

The search shelters at Juxtaposed locations are in use continuously 24/7, 365 days a year.  Over time dirt, dust and grease build up over the interior surfaces.  Whilst some shelters have ventilation, particulates from vehicle engines settle and build up on surfaces such as walls, floors and ceilings.

Therefore industrial cleaning services shall be required for the mandatory cleaning of the fabricated shelter interior by high pressure jet washing or suitable cleaning methods.

This opportunity is open to suppliers from the United Kingdom and Europe.

title: ITIP Bus Shelters (Supply and Install)
description:
Cornwall Council is delivering the One Public Transport System for Cornwall (OPTSfC) Project to create a single integrated public transport network.  A key driver and objective of the project is: to provide a high quality, accessible and integrated public transport network, developed to increase patronage on bus and rail and bring about an upturn in revenue income and service sustainability.

Through the OPTSfC project, Cornwall Council is working in partnership with Cormac Solutions on an Integrated Transport Improvement Programme (ITIP) to invest in numerous bus stop upgrades, improvements to stop locations and bus shelter provision to enhance the facilities for the public.  Cornwall Council's ambition for improvements to the quality and extent of the bus network required across the County also needs to be complemented by necessary and appropriate high quality roadside infrastructure.  

Corserv on behalf of Cormac Solutions is therefore seeking to appoint a suitably qualified and experienced manufacturer to produce, deliver and install, as may be required in varying volumes, bus shelters in Cornwall in support of the Integrated Transport Improvement Programme.  The appointed manufacturer will be required to provide shelters, as may be specified, by authorised representatives of Cormac Solutions.  Whilst there are no guaranteed quantities or expenditure commitments, shelters are required to primarily meet the needs of the Council's ITIP but will also cover any wider schemes and projects that Cormac Solutions and/or Cornwall Council has to deliver such as s278 works or s106 funding works etc as and when they arise.

So far as part of the ITIP Cormac Solutions has improved almost 300 bus stops and is currently engaged in the design and consultation of approximately 200 sites, several of which will require new shelters. The ITIP is likely to continue until the entire infrastructure of Cornwall's transport network has been totally modernised. 

Potential bidders must be able to meet the full scope and requirements of this tender opportunity. The intention is to appoint the most competitively priced bidder.

title: RBWM Bus Shelter Advertising
description:
The Royal Borough of Windsor and Maidenhead (RBWM) is seeking to engage with potential media partners for advertising concessions on its bus shelters, commencing from Autumn 2025. This opportunity involves the right to sell and display advertising on bus shelters that have the appropriate planning permission across the borough.

RBWM's asset list includes approximately 867 bus stops, with potential opportunities for both static and digital advertising post assessment. Currently, 38 of our shelters feature traditional format static 6-sheet posters. The selected supplier will be responsible for the posting and scheduling of adverts, as well as the installation and maintenance of all advertising assets. This includes both traditional poster panels and digital networks, where awarded.

While the maintenance and cleaning of the bus shelters are currently managed by third parties, RBWM will be looking to work with the selected supplier on options available for the installation, maintenance, and cleaning of the advertising panels on the bus shelters.

RBWM has yet to be decide the delivery model to be used, including the use of lots, contract length, preferred revenue model. Therefore the purpose of this Preliminary Market Engagement is to provide initial feedback to assist with determining this outcome.

All questions are not mandatory, however RBWM encourages completion of all questions (if necessary) in order to make an informed and thorough decision.

Questions five and six pertain to a separate asset list. This list, containing sensitive information, is available upon request and should not be distributed or shared.

Please use this link https://forms.office.com/e/QgCMhrSm76 to complete the questionnaire.

This link will divert you to questionnaire. Please contact aaron.fernandes@RBWM.gov.uk to request the asset list.

title: Fleming Way Improvements Bus Shelters
description:
Swindon Borough Council invites tenders from suitably qualified and experienced contractors for the provision new bus shelters to service 27 bus stops on a new public transport interchange for Swindon town centre.  The redevelopment of Fleming Way is part of the wider town centre regeneration works and aims to create one inclusive bus interchange that will accommodate all bus services in the town centre.  

The aspiration is to provide low carbon bus shelters in terms of manufacturing processes, materials, lifecycle of the product, and eventual recycling of materials.

The bus shelters must be of high quality, contemporary but simple design, and something for Swindon to be proud of.

The bus shelters should be as environmentally friendly as possible in their manufacture, materials and whole life costs.  It is essential to provide units which are low carbon (net zero if possible), with sustainable methods of construction and established 'green' company credentials, in line with the ethos of this project.

Full details relating to the provision of the bus shelters are supplied in the tender documents.

To participate, Suppliers will need to Register as a Supplier with ProContract, www.supplyingthesouthwest.org.uk, then Register an Interest before obtaining access to the tender documents. In the event of difficulties registering as a supplier, please refer to the System Administrator Proactis support team ProContractSuppliers@proactis.com

Contract Period
The contract will be initially for a period of 10 months commencing 20th February 2023 as per the contract terms and conditions. 

Tender Responses
Tender Responses are required to be submitted no later than 25th November 2022, 1200 hrs UK Local time via ProContract tender portal - https://www.supplyingthesouthwest.org.uk/

Tender Clarifications
If you require any clarifications on the tender documents, please submit these in writing via Messaging in ProContract tender portal by 4th November 2022, 1200 hrs UK Local time. The Council's responses to these clarification questions will be uploaded periodically on the ProContract tender portal. 

Please note that this project has been advertised on Find a Tender Service and Contracts Finder however, Supplying the South West Portal, https://www.supplyingthesouthwest.org.uk/ is the only portal that includes all the relevant documents required to respond to this opportunity, bearing Project Id: DN635685

No Tender submissions will be considered without completing the tender documents in full including attachments and returning them in alignment with the instructions within the tender documents.
