title: North Northamptonshire Council Sheltered Housing Provision of Digital Alarm Stock
description:
North Northamptonshire Council (NNC) is inviting Expressions of Interest (EOI) from potential suppliers for the delivery of digital alarms across the Council's Sheltered Housing. NNC are looking to put in place measures in order ensure our Sheltered Housing Alarm stock continues to work post the National switchover of phone lines from analogue to digital.

After reviewing options available, we have determined that the route we wish to take is upgrading our existing systems, as opposed to any form of Analogue adaptors.

We are also building an in-house team for alarm monitoring. Therefore, our future potential procurement will be split into two lots.

Lot 1 - Provision of Sheltered Housing (grouped living) alarms. 
Lot 2 - Provision of digital tele-alarm monitoring software. 

The aim of this pre-market engagement is to help us understand elements such as cost, and timescales for deployment, among other things.

Our Sheltered Housing units are split across two localities (Kettering and Corby). There are 10 sheltered housing schemes in the Kettering area, and 27 in Corby. There are circa 972 individual properties within these 37 combined schemes.

Each property holds a number of different systems including:

•	Speech Modules 
•	Personal Trigger / Fall pendants 
•	Pull cords
•	Door entry systems
•	Alarm control panels (that connect heat/smoke detectors and inform us if one activates)
•	Lift call systems
•	Communal break glass

The supplier's equipment will be required to operate within both an urban and rural setting where the quality and level of internet connectivity may vary. Interoperability with peripherals from other manufacturers would be advantageous as we look at future phases of preventative care.

Suppliers' equipment will be required to use the following digital protocols SCAIP, TS50134-9, or BS8521-2 with devices being compatible and interoperable with the digital Alarm Receiving Centre (ARC) technology platform, which is yet to be confirmed. 

We are progressing with the development of an in-house monitoring service and as such are looking at market options for the supply of a cloud-based monitoring software for our Community Alarm and Sheltered Housing users. This is why we are keen to understand compatibility with different Alarm Receiving Centre (ARC) platforms.

To submit your interest, please read the document attached to this notice and respond by answering the relevant questions. 
Please send all correspondences to
procurement@northnorthants.gov.uk by 13th September 2024.

title: Rainout Shelters Running on Rails
description:
The University of Nottingham is long established as a leader in plant and crop science research. As part of the future food beacon of excellence, the University is investing in its research infrastructure and wishes to commission a rain out shelter. The purpose of the rain out shelter is to be able to manipulate growing conditions,in the field, to simulate drought at specific stages of crop development. For example, to exclude any rainfall during the grain filling period of wheat. To enable this, we wish to commission shelters that respond to rain and only move into position when needed. This means that crops grow in natural, field conditions, for the majority of their growth and are only covered when it is actually raining. The shelters must, therefore, be self-propelled and able to move into position in response to rain. It is important that they are sufficiently portable to be able to be moved between fields to avoid problems with crop rotation.

For further information refer to OJEU contract notice 2019/S 079-189519 or the University's e-tendering portal. https://in-tendhost.co.uk/universityofnottingham/aspx/Home.

description:
Milton Keynes Council is seeking expressions of interest from companies who are able to offer bus shelter supply and maintain contracts. 
The Council would seek to include some element of advertising, including digital advertising, as well as green (planted) and solar roofing options. We would be seeking high quality purpose built bus shelters that could offer static and digital information displays dependant on the local situation. Shelters would need to be able to reuse existing Real time information panels where present. At present Milton Keynes has approximately 1000 stops with around 500 shelters. We would be seeking to retain this level of coverage. Some stops would have enhanced infrastructure which might include integrated cycle parking and other innovations.

title: Self-Propelled Rainout Shelter
description:
The University of Nottingham invites tenderes for a design and build contract for up to four self-propelled rainout shelters:
The purpose of the rainout shelter is to be able to manipulate growing conditions, in the field, to simulate drought at specific stages of crop development. For example, to exclude any rainfall during the grain filling period of wheat. To enable this, we wish to commission shelters that respond to rain and only move into position when needed. This means that crops grow in natural, field conditions, for the majority of their growth and are only covered when it is actually raining. The shelters must, therefore, be self-propelled and able to move into position in response to rain.
It is important that they are sufficiently portable to be able to be moved between fields to avoid crop rotation.

For more information the OJEO Contract Notice reference is 2018/S 224-512161 and the link to the University's e-tendering website to download the tender documents is https://www.in-tendhost.co.uk/universityofnottingham .

title: UK SBS PS150219 Space Mission
description:
Innovate UK is the UK’s innovation agency. Its goal is to accelerate economic growth by stimulating and supporting business-led innovation. Innovate UK is a government agency, based in Swindon and sponsored by the Department for Business, Innovation and Skills (BIS). Innovate UK brings together business, research and the public sector, supporting and accelerating the development of innovative products and services to meet market needs, tackle major societal challenges and help build the future economy. It delivers its objectives through a series of programmes, in which businesses are encouraged to participate in, such as Collaborative Research & Development, Smart, and Knowledge Transfer Partnerships, and, in order to help businesses internationally, Entrepreneur Missions. This specification relates solely to the Entrepreneur Missions programme. Innovate UK supports events known as ‘Entrepreneur Missions', run in partnership with UKTI, Catapults and private sector partners, in which a selected group of innovative and often early-stage UK companies are given intensive support to get them ready to travel to countries strong in innovation and enterprise, such as the US, Brazil or India.
The number of Entrepreneur Missions run by Innovate UK is currently small – 3 to 4 every year. As a result, the organisation has decided not to manage Entrepreneur Missions out of core resources, but to contract out the preparation and project management of the missions and logistical support before, during and after, the missions.
Each mission is therefore delivered in partnership with a private sector contractor to provide this expertise. The aim of the current tender, therefore, is to procure the services of a private sector provider to support the next Entrepreneur Mission in the Space sector, to the US market in November 2015.

The main objective of this and all other Entrepreneur Missions is to grow the participating businesses through early internationalisation. 
Specific objectives are for the mission companies to
1.	Raise investment
2.	Generate sales leads
3.	Establish new connections

title: UK SBS PS150142 Innovate UK Clean and Cool Missions
description:
Executive Summary
This contract will be for a five year programme to act the Delivery Partner for Innovate UK activities helping young businesses in the cleantech sector access overseas markets and investment. The activities will be known as the Clean and Cool Programme and comprise of running Clean and Cool Missions (two during the five year period of this contract), managing the network of Mission alumni companies including work to track their performance, and growth of the Clean and Cool Network, particularly from Innovate UK’s own funding alumni.
Clean and Cool Missions are entrepreneur missions taking 15-20 young, innovative UK cleantech companies to overseas markets to rapidly build their networks, know-how and ambition as they forge new business. Places on the Missions are competitive and applicants must demonstrate a scalable cleantech solution, a strong team and a fit with the market in the Mission location.
The Clean and Cool Network comprises Mission alumni companies, promising UK cleantech SMEs with the potential to be future Mission companies and Mission stakeholders such as sponsors, advisors, investors and journalists.
Clean and Cool Missions take 15-20 of the UK’s best young, innovative, high-growth potential companies to overseas markets to help them grow faster. Missions achieve this by supporting the SMEs to:
•	Network with potential customer, partners and investors 
•	Improve their Profile through PR coverage
•	Refine their Pitch to potential investors and to suit the target market
•	Gain Insight into what it takes to do business in the target market
•	Raise their Ambition and accelerate their plans

noticeIdentifier: UK SBS PS16104 - Connected Cities Mission to India
title: UK SBS PS16104 - Connected Cities Mission to India
description:
This contract is for an organisation to act as the Delivery Partner for Innovate UK activities to help young businesses in the smart cities/urban living sector access the Indian market and raise investment there. The aim of the exercise is to find a partner who is able to work effectively with start-ups and SMEs to prepare them to take part successfully in an overseas trade mission, and to maximise the opportunities that such participation will offer.
The mission is intended to be run in partnership with UKTI as a hybrid of a standard trade mission and an Innovate UK entrepreneur mission. It will take up to 20 young, innovative UK companies to India to build rapidly their networks, know-how and ambition as they forge new business. Places on the Mission will be competitive and applicants must demonstrate a scalable solution to a complex urban challenge, a strong team, and a fit with the market in India. The balance of the places on the mission will be taken up by larger more established firms who might also be potential clients for the entrepreneurs.
Objectives
This Connected Cities mission will take up to 20 of the UK’s best young, innovative, high-growth potential companies to overseas markets to help them grow faster. The Mission will achieve this by supporting the SMEs to:
•	Network with potential customers, partners and investors 
•	Improve their profile through PR coverage
•	Refine their pitch to potential investors and to suit the target market
•	Gain insight into what it takes to do business in the target market
•	Raise their ambition and accelerate their plans
This support and experience is hard for young companies to gain. The Mission will also open doors that would be closed to a SME alone. The attraction of a competitive group backed by government helps Missions to engage critical partners in the overseas market.
This support will be funded by Innovate UK.
As an outcome of the support provided by the Delivery Partner, participating companies will:
1. Understand how to pitch their products/services compellingly to prospective investors and to prospective clients
2. Understand the market conditions of the countries being visited, including public procurement rules
3. Understand the experience of other UK entrepreneurs in entering the markets in question 
4. Maintain their enthusiasm for participation from first contact through to participation
5. Be able to meet and develop relationships with the other participating companies prior to the mission
There is separately a requirement for organisation and project management of the mission. If the mission is to be delivered in partnership with UKTI, then the Delivery Partner will need to work closely with their delivery partner, Innovision on these aspects, with oversight is from Innovate UK. Criteria for company selection are set in collaboration between Innovate UK and UKTI.

title: Knowsley Housing Trust - Customer Contact Solution
description:
THIS PROCEDURE HAS BEEN CANCELLED. KHT is seeking bids from service providers to provide an automated customer contact solution. The system will primarily be used to contact customers across the North West region, but may also be used wherever KHT has an operational requirement across the UK.

KHT requires the automated solution to deliver the following four modules:

- Debt collection outbound dialler module: A system that calls customers and provides the option to connect to a KHT member of staff so rent can be paid, or queries answered by the KHT team
- Tenant survey contacts module: A system that calls recipients to request the completion of a survey, and compiles responses
- Appointment setting and confirmation module: A system that calls customers to make and confirm appointments. Any request to rearrange an appointment is connected to a KHT member of staff
- Outbound broadcast module: A system that calls recipients to provide key messages to customers

The successful service provider must provide a single system that delivers all four elements, and will be required to implement, host and maintain the solution on behalf of KHT. The system must fully integrate with the KHT housing management system, Orchard.

All four elements will initially be required to be delivered via an automated voice service. The successful service provider must however have the capability to deliver the outbound broadcast module and appointment setting and confirmation module via SMS, since KHT may require this capability during the contract term.

KHT must have access to extract data from the system 24 hours a day, 365 days a year. The system must be able to contact KHT customers between the hours of 8:00AM and 8:00PM, Monday to Friday.

The total number of outbound calls required to be delivered by the system across all 4 modules is estimated to be between 100,000 and 122,500 per annum. The estimated breakdown of outbound calls across the four modules is a follows:

- Debt collection outbound dialler module: approximately 1,400 to 1,700 calls per month
- Tenant survey contacts module: approximately 6,000 to 7,000 calls per month
- Appointment setting and confirmation module: approximately 500 to 1,000 calls per month
- Outbound broadcast module: approximately 500 calls per month

Please note, during the contract term, the annual number of outbound calls associated with the tenant survey contacts and outbound broadcast modules could increase by approximately 25% per module. The annual number of outbound calls associated with the debt collection outbound dialler module could increase by approximately 100%. This is for information only, and KHT do not guarantee that any estimated call volume increases will apply during the contract term.

title: CONSULTANCY  SERVICES IN SUPPORT  OF A FUTURE CITIES  MISSION
description:
The Future Cities Catapult (the Catapult) SME Programme supports SME's working in the urban innovation sector to scale and grow. Helping SME's to access international markets around the world is a core feature of the programme.  
 
The Catapult is seeking a delivery partner to support the delivery of a UK Future Cities Mission Programme. The Future Cities Mission programme is an entrepreneurial trade mission that takes 20 young innovative UK companies to overseas markets to rapidly build their networks, know-how and investment opportunities.  
 
The services are dependent on the Catapult's successful tendering for Innovate UK's Future Cities Mission Programme. The successful Supplier would work with the Catapult under contract as a sub-contractor. 
 
The successful Supplier is to be included in the Catapult's tender proposal and if successful will work with the Catapult to deliver Innovate UK's Future Cities Mission Programme as a delivery partner.  
 
Responsibilities will include: 
 
•   Co-development of Mission goals, strategy and positioning 
•   Partner and stakeholder management 
•   Programme execution and ongoing coordination with partners 
•   Design of mission week agenda - identification, recruitment, briefing and management of hosts 
•   Event management of mission week 
•   Execution of the Mission week agenda 
•   Speaking on behalf companies and partners in briefing and networking events 
•   Competition processes for Mission company selection including promotion, webinars, management of applications, judging process, informing applications, ensuring integrity 
•   Ongoing management of companies: invoicing, Q&A, logistics, briefing, 1:1 support, coaching, providing them with promotional collateral 
•   Management of suppliers (recruitment, negotiation, briefing and payment)  •   Mission week logistics: booking and management of flights, coaches, accommodation, food and drink. 
•   selecting and recruiting high profile mentors for entrepreneurs (optional). 

This project will be led by the Catapult's SME Programme Team supported by in-house teams such as the Business Development Team, Communications Team, Events Team, as well as Market Analysts. 
 
The Supplier with domain expertise in communication, content and mission delivery will in the first instance work with Future Cities Catapult to establish clear roles and responsibilities and be flexible to refine them along the collaborative journey.

title: Quantum Technology Missions Roadmap Opportunity
description:
Introduction and summary of requirements 

We would like individuals or organisations with commercial and technical knowledge of the quantum technology sector to develop detailed road maps to support the design and delivery of the National Quantum Strategy Missions.  

The focus of each road map will vary based on the characteristics of each Mission, as well as current evidence gaps that need addressing to inform policy development. The breadth and depth of specialist expertise required to deliver the road maps is substantial, spanning technical milestones for quantum entanglement networks to the identification of clinical use cases for quantum sensors. This opportunity has therefore been broken down into four separate lots to allow individuals or organisations with specific expertise relevant to individual Missions to participate.  

Aims and Objectives 

This project will gather evidence to help DSIT understand the key commercial opportunities, technology requirements and milestones, and dependencies for each Mission. This evidence will inform ongoing policy development and help shape policy interventions such as technology-specific innovation programmes, infrastructure investments and market creation activity to support overall Mission delivery.  

Alongside common evidence requirements for all lots (technology development timelines and underpinning requirements for priority use cases), there are specific requirements for each. These are outlined below:

Lot A: Quantum networking (Mission 2): technology and research milestones to materialise quantum networks, analysis of competing quantum networking components, underpinning classical infrastructure requirements

Lot B: Quantum sensing for healthcare (Mission 3): high potential clinical use case identification, mapping of necessary activity through the NHS HealthTech adoption framework

Lot C: Quantum position, navigation and timing (PNT) (Mission 4): market size estimates for core sub-component technologies such as compound semiconductors, MEMS vacuum cells, integrated photonics and advanced packaging.

Lot D: Quantum sensing for critical national infrastructure (CNI) (Mission 5): Current and future performance of sensing platforms, market size estimates for use cases such as gravity sensing in different sectors

title: Early Engagement Notice Quantum Technology Missions Roadmapping
description:
In March 2023, the government published the National Quantum Strategy, where it committed to publishing long-term quantum missions to galvanise technology development towards ambitious outcomes. The 5 Quantum Missions were published in December last year.

The Department for Science Innovation and Technology (DSIT) would like individuals or organisations with commercial and technical knowledge of the quantum technology sector to develop detailed road maps to support the design and delivery of the each Quantum Mission. Road maps for each Mission will be contracted out separately through individual lots given the specialist expertise required to deliver each one (ranging from quantum networking infrastructure to medical technology adoption pathways). Road mapping activity for Mission 1 on quantum computing is not in scope.

Alongside common evidence requirements for all lots (technology development timelines and underpinning requirements for priority use cases), there are specific requirements for each. These are detailed below but will be refined throughout the pre-market engagement process and with the successful bidder(s):

Lot 1: Quantum networking (Mission 2): technology and research milestones to materialise quantum networks, analysis of competing quantum networking components, underpinning classical infrastructure requirements

Lot 2: Quantum sensing for healthcare (Mission 3): high potential clinical use case identification, mapping of necessary activity through the NHS HealthTech adoption framework

Lot 3: Quantum position, navigation and timing (PNT) (Mission 4): market size estimates for core sub-component technologies such as compound semiconductors, MEMS vacuum cells, integrated photonics and advanced packaging. 

Lot 4: Quantum sensing for critical national infrastructure (CNI) (Mission 5): Current and future performance of sensing platforms, market size estimates for  use cases such as gravity sensing in different sectors

title: UK SBS PS16216 Future Cities Mission Programme
description:
'*** This tender requirement was abandoned prior to a Contract award decision. ***'

Future Cities Missions take up to 20 of the UK's best young, innovative, high-growth potential companies to overseas markets to help them grow faster. Missions achieve this by supporting the SMEs to:
•	Network with potential customer, partners and investors 
•	Improve their Profile through PR coverage
•	Refine their Pitch to potential investors and to suit the target market
•	Gain Insight into what it takes to do business in the target market
•	Raise their Ambition and accelerate their plans

This support and experience is hard for young companies to gain. The Missions also open doors that would be closed to a SME alone. The attraction of a competitive group backed by government and private sponsors helps Missions to engage critical partners in the overseas market.
Future Cities Missions are funded by Innovate UK in partnership with UKTI. They are organised by an external Delivery Partner who project manage the considerable process and logistics as well as securing other sponsors and developing the programme. Oversight is from Innovate UK who also underwrite the Missions costs. Mission location and company selection are set in collaboration with Innovate UK and UKTI.

CLOSING TIME FOR BIDS IS 20/01/2017 11:00 HOURS. 
DO NOT APPLY DIRECTLY TO THE BUYER. 

To express your interest in this opportunity email ExpressionOfInterest@crowncommercial.gov.uk. To register on our e-sourcing portal click on https://gpsesourcing.cabinetoffice.gov.uk and follow the link "Register for CCS eSourcing"

title: AUD10099 - Innovating for Growth Start-Ups Programme - Maximising your Intellectual Property Module
description:
The British Library’s Business & IP Centre is designed to support small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and entrepreneurs from that first spark of inspiration to successfully launching and growing a business.

With funding support from the European Regional Development Fund, the Centre launched the ‘Innovating for Growth’ programme in late 2011 to focus on providing support to growth businesses, as opposed to start ups. Innovating for Growth – Phase 1 was completed in December 2015. Innovating for Growth - Phase 2 is due to start service delivery in May 2016, subject to final funding approval.

The ‘Innovating for Growth - Scale-ups’ programme aims to use the Centre’s unique resources and expertise to help high-growth businesses scale up, through developing new products and services and entering new markets. Each SME completes a number of face-to-face support modules through a combination of one-to-one meetings and workshops, which are tailored to the SME’s needs.

The face-to-face support, combined with practical workshops, is designed to accelerate each SMEs progress to the next stage. The Business & IP Centre’s information professionals alongside selected delivery partners will help each SME to put together a growth strategy and plan for their business, as well as identifying the workshops and one-to-one’s each SME will need to attend to hone their business knowledge and skills.

The module available for tender is: 

Maximising Your Intellectual Property

This is one of several modules that each SME needs to attend.
The Innovating for Growth - Scale-ups programme have been split into 7 modules as set out below. 

1.         Innovating for Growth Scale-Ups Programme - Building Your Brand Module 
2.         Innovating for Growth Scale-Ups Programme - Refining Your Business Model Module 
3.         Innovating For Growth Start-Ups Programme - Get Started Workshop Module 
4.         Innovating for Growth Scale Ups Programme - Developing A Growth Strategy Module 
5.         Innovating for Growth Scale-Ups Programme - Maximising Your Intellectual Property Module 
6.         Innovating for Growth Scale-Ups Programme - Creating A Marketing Strategy Module 
7.         Innovating For Growth Scale-Ups Programme - Product & Service Innovation Module 

If you are interested in participating in this tender please go to the British Library’s e-tendering portal at https://bl.bravosolution.co.uk, click on ‘View current opportunities and notices' link on the Home page and follow the on screen instructions in regards to registration and downloading the tender documents.

title: PS24227 - Critical analysis of post-mission disposal options for Cislunar space missions study (UKSAC24_0092)
description:
***The final date and time for the submission of bids has been extended to Wednesday 06th November @11:00***

DO NOT apply directly to the buyer.

All tender information MUST be submitted through the Jaggaer eSourcing Portal.

Brief Description of Requirement

UK Space Agency (UKSA)
 
Background

As a global leader in space sustainability and a strong investor in space exploration, the UK regularly engages in various national and international forums to help set norms and best practices that influence responsible behaviour for interplanetary and beyond Earth orbit missions. This includes membership of the Inter-Agency Space Debris Coordination Committee (IADC), United Nations Committee on the Peaceful Uses of Outer Space, the International Organization for Standardization (ISO), the International Astronautical Federation (IAF), among others. Given the sustainability challenges that the Cislunar environment presents, the UK Space Agency is keen to support the development of new guidelines for the sustainable use of Cislunar space and is therefore commissioning this study to feed directly into the aforementioned international forums. The outputs of this study may also be used by the UK Space Agency and wider UK Government to inform new regulatory policy for future UK-licensed lunar missions.
 
Inputs
-            UK Space Agency-commissioned report titled "Lunar Surface Disposal Study" by Capgemini, March 2024
-            IADC Action Item description - the UK Space Agency will endeavour to supply the Supplier with this document (subject to IADC agreement), which details the committee's internal work on mitigation and disposal options for lunar orbits
 
Aims and Objectives
The primary aim of this study is to deliver a report that supports the UK Space Agency in:
1.  Developing Cislunar regulatory policy regarding UK mission disposal options
2. Contributing to the IADC's development of Cislunar space debris mitigation guidelines

Please ensure you review all attached information to ensure a full understanding of this requirement. All attachments can be found with the Supplier Attachments tab within the Jaggaer eSourcing Portal. 

This contract will be awarded based on the evaluation criteria as set out in the  ITQ document. 

How to Apply

UK Shared Business Services Ltd (UKSBS) will be using the Jaggaer eSourcing Portal for this procurement. 

To register on the Jaggaer eSourcing portal please use the link https://beisgroup.ukp.app.jaggaer.com/ and follow the instructions to register as a supplier.

If you are already registered on the Jaggaer eSourcing Portal and wish to participate in this procurement, please use the link: https://beisgroup.ukp.app.jaggaer.com/.

Once you are logged into the system you will be able to locate the Procurement you wish to leave a bid on by clicking the ITTs Open to All Suppliers and searching for the reference number PS24227

title: UK SBS PS150228 'Entrepreneur Mission' to Foreign Markets
description:
This contract is for an organisation to act as the Delivery Partner for Innovate UK activities to help young businesses in the smart cities/urban living sector access overseas markets and investment. The aim of the exercise is to find a partner who is able to work effectively with start-ups and SMEs to prepare them to take part successfully in an overseas trade mission, and to maximise the opportunities that such participation will offer.
The mission will be run in partnership with UKTI as a hybrid of a standard trade mission and an Innovate UK entrepreneur mission. It will take 10-15 young, innovative UK companies (in a total delegation of 20 companies) to overseas markets to rapidly build their networks, know-how and ambition as they forge new business. Places on the Mission will be competitive and applicants must demonstrate a scalable smart cities solution, a strong team and a fit with the market in the Mission location. The balance of the places on the mission will be taken up by larger more established firms who might also be potential clients for the entrepreneurs.

This Smart Cities mission will take up to 15 of the UK’s best young, innovative, high-growth potential companies to overseas markets to help them grow faster. The Mission will achieve this by supporting the SMEs to:
•	Network with potential customer, partners and investors 
•	Improve their profile through PR coverage
•	Refine their pitch to potential investors and to suit the target market
•	Gain insight into what it takes to do business in the target market
•	Raise their ambition and accelerate their plans

title: UK SBS CS19236 - Future Cities Domestic Missions
description:
The final date and time for the submission of bids is Friday 23rd August  2019 11:00am.

DO NOT apply directly to the buyer.

All tender information MUST be submitted through the CCS eSourcing Portal.

The overall aim of the Domestic Missions programme is to catalyse and grow a market for Advanced Urban Services  built around city collaborations, creative strategic procurement and increased understanding between public authorities  and innovative SME businesses. 
This contract will be for a six month pilot programme to act as a Delivery Partner to Innovate UK activities. If the pilot programme proves to be a success and demonstrates impact there is the possibility that a further, more comprehensive, programme of Domestic Missions will be organised. 
The intention is to run five one day pilot Domestic Missions (2 in England, 1 in Scotland, 1 in Wales, 1 in N Ireland). Each Mission programme should be developed specifically to meet the needs of the selected area. Plans should be drawn up in collaboration with local public authorities, responding to specifically identified local priority challenges, particularly where funding could be identified to address these challenges. The Domestic Missions are not simply business pitching events. They are curated opportunities for businesses to understand authority challenges, constraints and processes better, and for authorities to understand the potential of emerging technologies.
The programme should build understanding and relationships both between and amongst, innovators and government agencies, with the intention to see Advanced Urban Services deployed broadly to address complex urban challenges.

Please ensure you review all attached information to ensure a full understanding of this requirement. All attachments can be found with the RFx attachments tab within the Emptoris System. 

This contract will be awarded based on the evaluation criteria set out in the ITQ document. 

How to Apply

As part of the strategic alliance between UK Shared Business Services Ltd (UK SBS) and Crown Commercial Service (CCS), UK SBS will be using the CCS eSourcing Suite for this procurement.

To register on the CCS eSourcing portal please follow the details contained within the Additional Information section of this notice.

If you are already registered on the CCS eSourcing portal and wish to register your interest in this opportunity please email; expressionofinterest@crowncommercial.gov.uk 

Your email must clearly state: 
•	the name and reference for the procurement you wish to register for; 
•	the name of the registered Bidder; 
•	the name and contact details for the registered individual sending the email.

title: Children's exposure to harmful content online - pilot study
description:
The estimated value given is for all 3 modules of the contract. The estimated module value is therefore up to c.£50,000 each

Ofcom wish to explore the feasibility of estimating the prevalence of (user generated) online harm for children on platforms which host user generated content including social media, gaming and search engines.

- We are interested in trialling a range of approaches to collecting this data, before we decide how we wish to gather this data in the future. However, we recognise that each approach will require different specialist skills. Consequently, we have split the requirement into three modules. 
Module 1: Undertaking survey research through schools / classrooms
Module 2: Undertaking the research using passive measurement of content viewed on web/ through apps 
Module 3: Other methods (open) to help understand what children see
- Tenderers can submit a proposal for one, two or all three of the modules. Tenderers must be clear within their proposal as to which modules they wish to be considered and evaluated for.  Each module will be evaluated and awarded separately by Ofcom.
- It is important that the output of each module enables us to compare/contrast the data with that gathered in the other modules to help us decide how to proceed. 
- Each Module will address the same objectives, but using different methodological approaches

title: Provision of Accreditation of Level 5 in Data Analytics
description:
ONS is seeking to appoint a provider to accredit a series of learning modules to support the development of analysts across government.
      The modules are based on the accredited former RSS Higher programme split into six modules, offered as a level 5 scheme that aligns with the recognised career pathway for statistical analysts.  The titles are as follows and the module content (copyright owned by ONS) is provided as appendices
      o Module 1 - Data collection and interpretation
      o Module 2 - Probability models
      o Module 3 - Basic statistical methods
      o Module 4 - Linear models
      o Module 7 - Time series and index numbers
      o Module 8 - Survey sampling and estimation
      The programme is run over two years, with three modules per academic year.  All sessions will be run by ONS staff, and we will provide the assignments and examination invigilation (under direction from the accrediting body).
      The supplier will be required to set level 5 examinations, mark examinations and provide a valid accreditation to students who pass, identifying any necessary adjustments within the content to bring the modules in line with academic requirements.

title: Pre-Reception CPD Programme - Personal, Social and Emotional Development Module
description:
The purpose of this early engagement notice is to notify the market of the Department for Education&apos;s (DfE) intention to undertake a procurement exercise to appoint a suitably experienced body to develop a high quality continuous professional development (CPD) online training module in Personal, Social and Emotional Development (PSED) to be made available to early years practitioners responsible for 2 to 4-year-olds. This will involve developing the module drawing on the best evidence on CPD on PSED to be delivered as an online training for early years practitioners and with supporting materials for the trainers. The module will be piloted and adapted based on the results. 
The Department is investing in CPD training in PSED to support children to recover lost learning as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic. Evidence shows that children's PSED has been negatively impacted by the pandemic.
The contractor will be responsible for designing a standalone module covering PSED for practitioners complementing other high quality CPD modules on early language, literacy and maths funded by the DfE. The module will mainly support practitioners to provide high quality support on children's PSED. It will also address practitioners' own wellbeing as well.  In addition, the contractor will produce training materials to be used by the trainers. We expect the resulting products to be suitable for up to a 3-day training programme and versatile to allow for different formats of delivery such as (but not limited to) online modules or webinars delivered by or on behalf of the Department.  The PSED module and training materials will be piloted robustly in PVI or school-based settings with a comparatively high proportion of disadvantaged children (receipt of early years pupil premium, 2 year old entitlement, EHC plans as well as Income Deprivation Affecting Children Index criteria).
Crown Commercial will own the intellectual property rights on the PSED module and training materials.   

Delivery of the PSED training is currently planned for the Spring Term of 2022.  The delivery of the training will not be covered by the contract. 
The following areas will be covered:
-	Consideration of the training needs of the early years workforce 
-	Issues that have been identified in children's PSED development (aged 2 to 4) as a result of the COVID-19 pandemic and how to address these drawing on the best evidence.
-	Consideration of the mental health needs of the workforce drawing on the best evidence 
-	Indicate in the bid how they will create and maintain a working relationship with DfE and any third parties throughout the lifetime of the contract.
We are seeking an organisation with expertise and experience of supporting the mental and emotional health of young children in the education sector and one that can demonstrate its track record of drawing on the latest evidence in the design of CPD programmes.

title: Safety and security advice and assistance for election observation missions
description:
1.STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURES/TOOLKIT: Develop a standard health, safety and security manual for observers (based on standard operating procedures and recommended minimum training standards), and a DIY risk assessment tool for all long or short term election observation missions for lower-risk environments. 
We anticipate this would be a fixed fee, one-off assignment. 

2.STRATEGIC ADVICE AND RISK ASSESSMENT: Deliver security consultancy and advice to WFD in advance of a proposed election observation mission, including a location-specific comprehensive risk assessment and security plan for a mission to a higher risk environment, upon request, for the purposes of proposals/bids or approved mission planning. 
We anticipate this could be structured as a retainer or a daily fee rate or quote per assignment, to be drawn down under the framework as required. 

3.MISSION-SPECIFIC ADVICE AND ASSISTANCE: Provision of a bespoke verbal pre-departure briefing; on-the-ground safety, security and logistical support as required (including local security awareness training, supply of close protection, guard services, etc. and COVID-19 related assistance, either directly or via a local partner); remote threat monitoring and 24/7 advice and assistance; and crisis support (in conjunction with WFD's insurance-related response consultants and/or the FCDO, as appropriate) for missions to high-risk environments. 
We anticipate this would be quoted per assignment, to be drawn down under the framework as required. 

Submitting a bid
In general, the bid should include the following:
-Organisational profile
-Proposed solution for each of the services
-Financial proposal
-References
-Confirmation of compliance with Conditions of Tendering
Organisational profile: 
•	Company profile, including brief history and financial overview
•	Case studies/credentials demonstrating relevant experience and skills profile 
•	Names and brief biographies of key staff 
•	Registered to a relevant and existing HMG framework agreement
WFD is particularly keen to receive bids from organisations which are - or are working towards becoming - living wage employers and that have a broadly representative and balanced Board from gender and ethnicity perspectives.  
Proposed solution: 
•	Clear explanation as to the proposed approach to meeting the specification set out in this ITT. 
•	Indicative service level agreement to facilitate the delivery of each service line, including timelines, assumptions and dependencies, resourcing and risks. 
Financial proposal: 
•	Full breakdown costings for the proposed solution for each service line
•	Separate accounting of VAT and/or any other applicable tax, duty, or charge. 
•	Any discount applied in view of WFD's not-for-profit status. 
References:
•	Bid should include two references relating to similar goods/services provided in the last 3 years.

Detailed information and evaluation criteria can be found in the ITT attached.

title: UK Export Academy's Agri Food and Drink modules - subject-specific masterclass
description:
The UK Export Academy (UKEA) from the Department for Business and Trade (DBT) is a free training programme for UK businesses of all sizes looking to grow their international sales.  So far over 16,000 businesses have signed up for the UK Export Academy. The sector specific modules were launched in 2022 with sector teams delivering &apos;Sector Overviews&apos;. This is a contract for the Food and Drink sector specialism .
 The sector is also highly regulated and trading can be complex, particularly for smaller businesses. Understanding these complexities is the key to successful exporting. We want to demystify the challenge.  
 There are not just barriers but also opportunities. Post Brexit, we want SMEs to utilise newly signed free trade agreements. Only one in five SMEs export, there is massive untapped potential for the UK&apos;s s largest manufacturing sector to export more. We want to encourage and inspire more businesses who only trade domestically to starting or re-start exporting, with our modules.  
 To respond to an increasing demand from our audience, We would be looking for bidders to develop and deliver various modules, via Live webinars, to businesses seeking to export across different markets. These would enable companies to confidently export and demystify complex requirements and pitfalls involved.

We are looking for:
- A curriculum of modules tailored and mapped to meet all relevant stages of exporting food and drink is required. The modules should be based on evidence from DBT and other robust sources to demonstrate that the topics and content align to the most significant capability challenges faced by SME's in the sector. 
- An inspiring UK-wide approach: information should be inclusive and engaging to different audiences from around the union and tailored as required to engage businesses from Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland. We want delegates to feel inspired about their comparative advantage: quality, innovation, traceability, heritage, sustainability, and high animal welfare standards. 
- Pedagogical tools: A teaching specialism, building on food and drink exporting experience, to help audience retain and act on the information. Quality assurance: The provider must have a food and drink specialism to ensure the content in relevant and that they can answer questions posed by companies. 
- Delivery: A consistent rhythm of quarterly modules that SMEs can reliably access. The modules can be delivered on a quarterly basis, with a minimum of 3 modules delivered per quarter, and a maximum of 16 per year. We would like the winning bidder to commence the development of the off er in Spring 2024. Supplier will need to be contracted by 6 May 2024. Module delivery will need to commence from May 2024.

title: NC RFI011 - NavyPODS (Deployable Mission Modules)
description:
The Ministry of Defence (the Authority) welcomes responses to this Request for Information (RFI) in order to understand the 'art of the possible' in regards to the potential future creation of a number of deployable mission modules, known as NavyPODS (Navy Persistent Operational Deployment System).

Specifically, we are looking for the best ideas, concepts and available technologies from industry and other organisations to inform us of the potential capabilities that could be suitable for modular deployment on a range of current and future platforms. 

Each deployable mission module would be expected to take the form of an ISO equivalent container containing a system, or multiple systems, that contribute towards a mission specific capability. 

Please see the attached document for full details.

title: Dementia Mission Market Engagement Day
description:
The Dame Barbara Windsor Dementia Mission Market Engagement Day hosted by OLS will take place on 17th March 2023 at 10:00am-12:00pm

The Life Sciences Vision published by the Office for Life Sciences (OLS) in July 2021 identified "improving translational capabilities in neurodegeneration and dementia" as one of its flagship healthcare mission challenges. On the 14th August 2022 the Prime Minister launched the Dame Barbara Windsor Dementia Mission, along with £95m funding. This funding was reaffirmed in a further announcement on the Healthcare Missions on the 28th November 2022. To deliver this mission, OLS intend to develop innovative research tools and boost the number and speed of clinical trials in dementia and neurodegeneration.

You must register for the event here https://forms.office.com/e/UcMYDrLpNh.

We will be testing the proposals to deliver the mission at the market engagement day, where your input would be greatly valued. Please still register through the link even if you cannot attend the day as it will be recorded, and we will be sending out the slides, a Q&A from the day and a survey asking details around your interest in the dementia mission to all individuals who register through the link.

Additional information is available in the attached document. Please use the link below

https://atamis-1928.cloudforce.com/sfc/p/0O000000rwim/a/8d000001QbkW/2rNKu7mfqKJwe9BF.MFHUT6drgUyM9B6_h.GhZ2LRAc

title: Innovate UK PR Support of Innovate UK Entrepreneur Missions 2018/19
description:
CLOSING TIME FOR BIDS IS 28/02/2018 14:00 HOURS. DO NOT APPLY DIRECTLY TO THE BUYER.

Innovate UK's communications team is prioritising four of its overseas Entrepreneur Missions in 2018-2019, one per quarter, to inspire businesses to engage with its toolkit of support and to build content and coverage that support its thought leadership on innovation and global opportunities for the UK.

A PR agency with an international network and offices in London and in destination cities is required to package and sell-in each of these chosen 4 Entrepreneur Missions, with the core aim of achieving substantial in-country, international and national broadcast and print coverage.
Entrepreneur Missions have excellent potential for a PR package that delivers national, trade and broadcast coverage. Each has a clear narrative structure of beginning, middle and end; a cast of companies or characters; a collective motivation; a global opportunity. Each is a journey with clear destination and timeline.

Entrepreneur Mission PR plans should look to exploit this structure with Innovate UK and its spokespeople positioned as the conveners and thought leaders of the journey and the participating companies as the best of British innovation in the relevant sector, that have hard-won a special opportunity to Go Global.

noticeIdentifier: RSSB2552 - Development of an NTS interactive eLearning package to supplement learning delivery media
title: RSSB2552 - Development of an NTS interactive eLearning package to supplement learning delivery media for the RSSB Train Driver Training Course
description:
RSSB has recently delivered a project on behalf of the Rail Delivery Group (RDG), which has resulted in the development of a blended learning, fully interactive train driver learning programme. This course has been piloted with a number of railway undertakings. Following the initial course feedback, a number of eLearning resources have been developed to support the course and an additional eLearning resource for NTS has been requested and this document specifies the requirements for this. 
Currently, the Non-Technical Skills (NTS) content is delivered on two separate days and it is envisioned that some of this learning can be delivered through an eLearning solution, allowing the two classroom days to focus on the contextualising of this NTS learning in an operational environment. While this new eLearning resource will initially be used in conjunction with the train driver training programme it must also be developed so it can be utilised by other safety critical roles. The eLearning package needs to applicable to a number of audiences and these include; 
•	RSSB Train Driver Programme delegates 
•	Attendees of the RSSB NTS course 
•	Trainers or briefers, prior to using RSSB T1064 material


Deliverables

The key deliverable of this project is:
•	To create and develop an NTS interactive eLearning module with associated assessment; to be held and accessed through the current VLE and RSSB extranet as a complete self-paced learning package.

The key project tasks are:
1.	With RSSB agree learning objectives for the eLearning module
2.	Work with RSSB technical specialists to define the structure and content for the NTS eLearning module
3.	Manage the development and selection of content for authoring and filming 
4.	Create storyboards from the key learning points for the module
5.	Design and create the assessment techniques appropriate to the content and learning objectives along 
6.	Specify data storage arrangements
7.	If required professionally film agreed video content.  This is expected to last no longer than one day of filming


1.	The eLearning package should cover the basics of HF, Human information processing, human error and introduce NTS. Potential topics and modular structure could be:
•	Module 1 - What is HF and why do people need to consider human performance?
•	Module 2 - How do people process information, make decisions and act - the OUDA model?
•	Module 3 - What affects our processing, decision making and action, and how can this lead to human error or violations?
•	Module 4:  - What are the different types of human errors and violation?
•	Module 5 - What are non-technical skills and how they can help reduce errors and violations?
•	Module 6: What do non-technical skills look like? - positive and negative demonstrations

2	The eLearning package should provide links to additional reading and resources. 
3	The content must be meaningful to all key rail safety critical roles.

title: British Library Innovating for Growth - Scale-ups Programme
description:
The British Library’s Business & IP Centre is designed to support small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and entrepreneurs from that first spark of inspiration to successfully launching and growing a business.

With funding support from the European Regional Development Fund, the Centre launched the ‘Innovating for Growth’ programme in late 2011 to focus on providing support to growth businesses, as opposed to start ups. Innovating for Growth – Phase 1 was completed in December 2015. Innovating for Growth - Phase 2 is due to start service delivery in May 2016, subject to final funding approval.

The ‘Innovating for Growth - Scale-ups’ programme aims to use the Centre’s unique resources and expertise to help high-growth businesses scale up, through developing new products and services and entering new markets. Each SME completes a number of face-to-face support modules through a combination of one-to-one meetings and workshops, which are tailored to the SME’s needs.

The face-to-face support, combined with practical workshops, is designed to accelerate each SMEs progress to the next stage. The Business & IP Centre’s information professionals alongside selected delivery partners will help each SME to put together a growth strategy and plan for their business, as well as identifying the workshops and one-to-one’s each SME will need to attend to hone their business knowledge and skills.

The Innovating for Growth - Scale-ups programme have been split into 7 modules as set out below. Please note the Get Started Workshop module (AUD10071) will be published on Monday 14 March 2016:

1.	Innovating for Growth Scale-Ups Programme - Building Your Brand Module (AUD10069)
2.	Innovating for Growth Scale-Ups Programme - Refining Your Business Model Module (AUD10070)
3.	Innovating For Growth Start-Ups Programme - Get Started Workshop Module (AUD10071) - **published 14 March**
4.	Innovating for Growth Scale Ups Programme - Developing A Growth Strategy Module (AUD10072)
5.	Innovating for Growth Scale-Ups Programme - Maximising Your Intellectual Property Module (AUD10073)
6.	Innovating for Growth Scale-Ups Programme - Creating A Marketing Strategy Module (AUD10074)
7.	Innovating For Growth Scale-Ups Programme - Product & Service Innovation Module (AUD10075)

If you are interested in participating in this tender please go to the British Library’s e-tendering portal at https://bl.bravosolution.co.uk, click on ‘View current opportunities and notices' link on the Home page and follow the on screen instructions in regards to registration and downloading the tender documents.

title: Department for Education Specification: Pedagogy Experts to Create Training Modules for Relationships, Sex and Health Education (RSHE)
description:
The Authority wants to provide teachers with a single point of access to online training modules on RSHE that are aligned to the areas that teachers have told us, through research that they find most challenging. These training modules will be published alongside other support materials for schools such as the implementation guide, case studies and quality assured resources.
The topics below have been identified by teachers as areas they find most challenging to teach and therefore require training. There may be other areas that schools identify as a training need when we begin to test the modules with a wider pool of schools during development. The Contractor shall work with the Authority's digital content developer to design the training modules set out in the specification for use in schools. The Pedagogy Expert (contractor) will work with the DfE to oversee the design of training modules and ensure the training modules have a consistent pedagogical approach in line with those described in the Early Career Framework e.g. the training modules must provide facts and information that enables teachers to build on pupils' prior knowledge, break down complex material, and sequence knowledge in a coherent way. This includes enabling teachers to teach about the key knowledge and facts about the subjects.

SEND Experts to create training modules for Relationships, Sex and Health Education (RSHE) The SEND Expert (Contractor) shall work with a team of digital content developers and user researchers commissioned by the DfE to create training modules. These training modules will be aimed at equipping teachers to effectively teach the RSHE curriculum to pupils with SEND in special schools. This includes enabling teachers to teach about the key knowledge and facts about the subjects.
The purpose of the SEND experts review is to provide children and young people with the knowledge to lead safe, healthy and happy lives. Some of the statutory content will need to be handled with particular care and sensitivity, for example on safeguarding and mental health, and the Authority is committed to supporting high quality teaching.
As part of this commitment, the Authority wants to provide teachers with a single point of access to online training modules that are aligned to the areas that teachers have told us, through research that they find most challenging. These training modules will be published alongside other support materials for schools such as the implementation guide, case studies and quality assured resources.

description:
In this present instance, ESPO is working on behalf of Leicestershire County Council to undertake a procurement process to identify and appoint a number of Service Providers to supply training in respect of Positive Behaviour Support (PBS).

The requirement will be divided into the following nine lots:
• Module 1 – Safe Working Practices for Lone Workers
• Module 2 – Understanding Behaviours that Challenge
• Module 2A – Understanding Behaviours that Challenge Annual Refresher
• Module 3 – Understanding Behaviours that Challenge in Foster Care 
• Module 3A – Understanding Behaviours that Challenge Annual Refresher
• Module 4 – Understanding Behaviours that Challenge in Children’s Residential Services 
• Module 4A – Understanding Behaviours that Challenge in Children’s Residential Services Annual Refresher
• Module 5 – Positive Behaviour Support Planning
• Module 6 – Restrictive Interventions and Break away techniques

The framework will be open to the following public sector organisations throughout Leicestershire, Leicester City and Rutland:
• Local Authority Councils; 
• Educational Establishments (including Academies); 
• Central Government Departments and their Agencies;
• NHS and HSC Bodies, including Ambulance Services; 
• Registered Charities; 
• Registered Social Landlords

The framework agreement has the option to extend for up to a further 24 months. The total estimated values stated include the option period.

title: Advanced Mission Planning Aid-CF-VTN
description:
Voluntary Transparency Notice for Advance Mission Planning Aid (AMPA)

This is a Voluntary Transparency Notice to place a Single Source Contract for Advance Mission Planning Aids (AMPA).
The Command and Control, 2 Targeting Navigations and Communications Team (C2TNC DT), part of the Ministry of Defence ("the Authority"), intends to place a two-year contract extension with Enterprise Services Defence and Security UK Limited (DXC Technology) for the Advanced Mission Planning Aid (AMPA) ("the Contract"). The AMPA In-Service Support (ISS) contract provides Design Organisation support and Post Design Services for the two variants which because of their commonality realises an economy of scale benefit.
Due to mission data generated by AMPA being referenced when airborne, the system is deemed to impact air safety and the supplier, Enterprise Services Defence and Security UK Limited (DXC Technology), is required to be accredited by the Military Aviation Authority (MAA) as a Design Organisation (DO). Enterprise Services Defence and security UK Limited (DXC Technology) are the only company that design/ modify mission planning systems. Enterprise Services Defence and Security UK Limited are responsible for the in-service support and post design enhancement of the mission planning system.
The Authority has determined that this Single Source contract is excluded from Defence and Security Public Contract under Regulations 6(3A) (b) and 7(1) (a) of the DSPR 2011.

title: Module Evaluation System - AWARD
description:
The University of Cumbria (UoC) is seeking a new online Module Evaluation System to strengthen institutional mechanisms for gathering and responding to student feedback. UoC operates across a range of campuses in the North West and London, alongside a growing portfolio of online learning provision. 
      The Module Evaluation System will be used by all students to provide feedback on their experiences at module level. Additional surveys may be offered at programme level or to specific cohorts of students. The new Module Evaluation System will integrate with the existing UoC digital ecosystem, including SITS and Blackboard Ultra. The new system will be managed by the Centre for Academic Practice Enhancement (CAPE) with support from IT and Academic Registry, and will operate across all academic Institutes (equivalent of faculties).
      The new Module Evaluation System will play a key role in our new University approach to student engagement and communication through providing actionable insight into the student experience across all provision. It is intended that the first Module Evaluation Survey utilising the new system will run in November 2024.

Within the LGT Clinical Academy (LGTCA), there are 20 credit rated university level work based learning modules.  These range from a suite of preceptorships for Nurses, Midwives and Allied Healthcare professionals to surgical, medical, cancer and other specialist courses.  The LGTCA have on average 450 enrolled
     The LGTCA has on average 450 enrolled learners per financial year.  Each course is being learners per financial year. Each course is being offered at either level 6 or level 7, not offered at both. University Credits are different from module to module ranging from 15 to 30 credits depending on length and contact time of the module. As well as the overall service need building to a larger programme.
The LGTCA predominantly caters for Nursing workforce. We are looking to build and create more educational content for Midwives and Allied Healthcare professionals as well as looking at clinical leadership modules and senior leadership modules.
We are looking to expand further and sell some of our unique courses to the external London market. These will be based around the clinical subject matter experts that we have within the NHS organisation.

title: Launch Academy Modules
description:
ORE Catapult's Launch Academy is a transformative technology accelerator for the offshore wind sector. It will deliver a bespoke nine-month programme that is a truly unique offering for companies looking to break into the burgeoning industry. The Launch Academy will help enable companies to launch new technologies and services into the offshore wind market to enhance the UK supply chain, enabling greater UK content and supporting cost reduction through innovation. Launch Academy is delivered to cohorts of up to 10 companies at a time through a series of modules. Some of these modules are delivered by ORE Catapult, some are delivered by professional services companies on a pro-bono basis and some are delivered by 3rd party experts. This procurement exercise is seeking to appoint experts to deliver some of these modules. This procurement exercise shall cover the delivery of up to three (3) cohorts of Launch Academy., However, ORE Catapult reserves the right to reduce the number of cohorts delivered under this contract if ORE Catapult does not run as many cohorts as anticipated.
ORE Catapult has determined that, due to the nature of the requirements, it has been deemed appropriate to split this requirement into the following three (3) Work Packages:

Work Package 3 - Value Proposition Module
Tenderers are invited to submit proposals for any one (1), combination of or all Work Packages

noticeIdentifier: UK SBS PR17012 2 x Generic Instrument Modules
title: UK SBS PR17012 2 x Generic Instrument Modules
description:
Assembly of two EMSO Generic Instrument Modules:
Assembly and testing of two oceanographic instrument modules, including construction of the frame, capable of deployment in deep water at European Multidisciplinary Seafloor and water column Observatory (EMSO) locations.

A prototype EMSO Generic Instrument Module (EGIM) is currently undergoing shallow water testing. The bidder will assemble two replicates of the prototype module to house a selection of instruments provided. The replicated modules will be capable of deployment in water depths to 5000 m for periods of more than 1 year between servicing.

Components are procured in advance and the bidder will take receipt of the components from NOC, assemble and test the systems in accordance with accompanying dry‐ and wet-test acceptance guidelines. Engineering drawings and related documentation will be provided. 

Delivery of two pressure‐qualified EGIMs and accompanying dry‐ and wet-test acceptance reports must be completed and delivered within 40 days of receiving the components for validation prior to deployments of sensor modules anticipated in the latter half of 2017. Delivery will be to NOC, Southampton, SO14 3ZH.

The possibility exists for the bidder to make use of 25 square metre work space at the National Oceanography Centre's Marine Robotic Systems Innovation Centre (MARSIC) for approximately £2170 per month. Cost excludes the use of tools and machinery. At additional cost the testing facilities including large and small pressure test chambers can be accessed.

Example technical drawings of the completed EGIM  can be found in the attachments.

description:
A contract was awarded for a Digital Skills Enhancement Project that provides support to the skills development of FE teachers and trainers.
This ITT was for e-learning development, building on the existing Free Digital Skills Enhancement Project Online Modules by developing extension modules for further skills development in Word, Excel and PowerPoint.
This was achieved through updating the Foundation's Just-in-time Excel and PowerPoint modules and developing a new module for Word
The Just-in-time Excel and PowerPoint modules were launched in 2009 and are based on Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint 2003 and 2007. It was required these modules be updated to Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint 2016

title: Public Health E-Learning modules build and subject matter expert content
description: The eLearning Provider is required to develop a suite (minimum of nine (9) roughly 20-minute modules) of high-quality, interactive self-paced eLearning modules. The eLearning modules will be targeted at staff working within educational settings from early years through to further education (as relevant). The eLearning modules will be presented to users in an integrated way via the Hampshire Health in Education website on the Authority's Learning Management System which will be used to host, track, and record online learning delivered through this new provision. The Provider will source the subject matter expertise required, design and build the modules, including alpha and beta testing phases. The provider will deliver the final eLearning modules to the Authority in an agreed suitable format.

title: Provision of Health & Safety eLearning Software
description:
26.  Description of the requirement
•	To provide Health and Safety Training via E-learning Modules with Health and Safety course content to populate "Learn" section of the Evotix Health and Safety system. To be compatible with the Evotix system.  
•	The software to be easily uploaded to the Evotix Learn system (SCORM files) 
•	To have a SCORM compliant learning management system for accessibility and structure for staff Health and Safety Learning. 
•	The H&S software content itself, to enable DVLA to meet all its legal requirements under the Health and Safety at Work Act 1974 and Management of Health and Safety at Work 1999 etc. 
•	Software provider to keep modules up to date and in line with current Health and Safety regulation, advice and guidance. 
We would be looking for the software training modules and content for the Evotix system to:  
•	To deliver the H&S training modules/content to all DVLA staff via the Evotix system. 
•	Staff to have unlimited access to the procured H&S training modules. (no need for credits or licences).  
•	Enable records of training completed by staff, to be kept via the Evotix system securely. This should be in line with the National Archive Guidance, GDPR, and Health and Safety Executive requirements. 
•	Software to provide a clear overview of staff up-take, progress, completed rate, and overdue training.    
•	As above, all staff to be able to access the training modules via the Evotix system, as required, without licences or credits for each of the modules. Each member off staff to be able to access each module, therefore DVLA staff has an estimated 5500 users with 1000 user buffer.
•	Staff to be able to refresh their training on change or when the H&S team assign training, for example for new staff to access the H&S training and content Or staff to re-take their training on change etc. 
•	 The solution must include Display Screen Equipment (DSE) assessments as part of the training offering, in line with HSE guidance, to support staff wellbeing and legal compliance  
•	Provide the Health and Safety team with technical support for the implementation and management of the training modules/content, and for training on admin and use of software.  
•	Be available through any browser. (SaaS solution, compatible from DVLA device i.e.  PC, laptop, mobile phone, tablet, handheld device etc) 
•	Be easy to use and user friendly, with a simple interface to promote to ensure completion by staff /all users.
•	To meet all DVLA relevant factor requirements, IT Cyber Security, Data Protection, and Information Assurance, requirements. 
•	The provider must demonstrate compliance with Cyber Essentials, a UK government-backed certification scheme developed by the National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC), which helps organisations protect against common internet-based cyber threats
cpvDescriptionExpanded: Software package and information systems

title: SBRI: Active Debris Removal Refuelling Mission feasibility study - Phase 1
description:
This is a Small Business Research initiative (SBRI) competition funded by the UK Space Agency.  

The aim of the competition is to undertake feasibility studies to refuel the National Active Debris Removal Mission and a commercial client/customer satellite.

The challenge to be addressed by the successful team(s) can be summarised as follows: 

•	Elaborate the requirements for a mission which meets goal 1 of the study i.e. capable of refuelling the UK ADR satellite in Low Earth Orbit (LEO)
•	Elaborate the requirements for a mission which meets goal 2 of the study i.e. capable of refuelling a commercial client/additional partner
•	Develop a baseline design that responds to the technical requirements - providing confidence that the mission is feasible within the technical, programmatic, and financial constraints, which also defines what those constraints may be. Identify risks and propose realistic mitigations, establish plans for further development in the subsequent phases of the mission lifecycle
Further details of the tasks needed to deliver this SBRI can be found within Annex B - Project Specification- Active Debris Removal- Refuelling Study Phase 1 Overview.

It is important that you review Annex B as projects that are unable to meet the tasks of the SBRI may score poorly or be viewed as out of scope.


A total of up to £2 million, inclusive of VAT, is allocated to this competition/phase 1. 

The Phase 1 feasibility study research and development (R&D) contracts will be up to £500,000, inclusive of VAT, for each project for up to 8 months. We expect to fund up to 4 projects.

title: FCG/017 Satellite and Mission Simulator
description:
Text to be corrected in the original notice:
        Place of text to be modified: II.1.2) Short description of the contract or purchase
        Instead of: on behalf of ARMY HQ, Defence Equipment & Support is seeking to procure a  modular, event driven fully data representative satellite simulator for use in design, development, integration, test and operation of space segment assets; this will be used in support of the ARTEMIS mission.
        
        ARTSIM, as the baseline deliverable, will provide a set of generic default satellite configurations which can be used for the analysis of a range of mission operations and capability objectives. The system must take a flexible, distributed architecture approach, providing for a range of spacecraft components to be simulated to a level of fidelity which can be determined by the team utilising the tool. The system shall utilise high quality GUI and animations to visualise satellite and orbit configurations. MOD require the capability to deploy ARTSIM as a stand-alone software process, running on a single machine, or as a VM deployed on a hypervisor, or as a cloud based application running in a container.
        
        Simulation of the satellite systems, subsystems and interfaces in both real-time and simulated time, will provide an operationally ready capability, which is able to represent, as simulation instances, a range of satellite implementations (including constellations), with a full range of orbit configurations.	ARTSIM shall enable user (local or remote) control of the simulation including setting parameters and starting/stopping the simulation. ARTSIM shall have a set of default underlying functions, but also enable user definition of system mathematical models including orbit function, satellite function and unit models.  ARTSIM shall include interface connectivity functionality, enabling connection to hardware through electrical interfacing to support in the loop testing via a test harness.  Software interfacing shall enable connection to external databases, networks or remote control i.e TT&C databases and external Check-out systems
        
        The ARTSIM product shall support the simulation of system level configurations in the different mission phases (off-launcher separation, and solar panel deployment), definition of the reference systems, the functional block diagrams of the satellite, payload and equipment layouts and functional paths.
        The ARTSIM product shall support the development and understanding of satellite level system budgets including:
        •	Mass   
        •	Inertial properties   
        •	Unit power consumption for all operational modes   
        •	Power available in different mission phases
        Read: Defence Equipment & Support is seeking to procure a  modular, event driven fully data representative satellite simulator for use in design, development, integration, test and operation of space segment assets; this will be used in support of the ARTEMIS mission.

title: Innovating for Growth Scaleups Programme
description:
The British Library's Business & IP Centre is designed to support small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and entrepreneurs from that first spark of inspiration to successfully launching and growing a business.

The Centre offers free access to the UK's most comprehensive collection of business and intellectual property information, including around 50 million patent specifications, unique databases on trademarks and registered designs, thousands of market research reports, company reports, trade journals, business directories and guides to legal information and government publications.  British Library information experts are on hand to guide users to the resources.  There is also a range of workshops and events run by British Library experts and partners.

With funding support from the European Regional Development Fund, the Centre launched the 'Innovating for Growth' programme in late 2011 to focus on providing support to growth businesses, as opposed to start ups. Innovating for Growth - Phase 1 was completed in December 2015. Innovating for Growth - Phase 2 started in May 2016 and a project extension is due to start service delivery in October 2019, subject to final funding approval.

The 'Innovating for Growth - Scale-ups' programme aims to use the Centre's unique resources and expertise to help high-growth businesses scale up, through developing new products and services and entering new markets.

There are nine tender opportunities under this project.

001304 Innovating for Growth Scaleups Programme 'Building Your Brand'
001305 Innovating for Growth Scaleups Programme 'Refining your Business Model' Module
001306 Innovating for Growth Scaleups Programme 'Get Started Workshop' Module
001307 Innovating for Growth Scaleups Programme 'Developing a Growth Strategy' Module
001308 Innovating for Growth Scaleups Programme 'Maximising Your Intellectual property' Module
001309 Innovating for Growth Scaleups Programme 'Deliver Introduction Workshop' Module
001310 Innovating for Growth Scaleups Programme 'Creating a Marketing Strategy' Module
001311 Innovating for Growth Scaleups Programme 'Product and Service Innovation' Module
001312 Innovating for Growth Scaleups Programme 'Financial Planning For Growth' Module

If you are interested in any of these opportunities please browse to https://bl.bravosolution.co.uk and click on the Current Opportunities and Notices link for further details
